Wenn du dich für Capgemini entscheidest, wählst du ein Unternehmen, in dem du die Möglichkeit hast, deinen Karriereweg selbst zu gestalten. Du wirst von einem kollaborativen Netzwerk von Kolleg*innen auf der ganzen Welt unterstützt und inspiriert und kannst neu definieren, was möglich ist. Schließe dich uns an und unterstütze die führenden Organisationen der Welt, den Wert von Technologie zu erschließen und eine nachhaltigere, inklusivere Welt zu gestalten.

Deine Rolle

Als Senior Data &
column2:
AI Engineer*in gestaltest du datengetriebene Lösungen entlang des gesamten Lebenszyklus – von der Integration über Transformation und Qualitätssicherung bis zur Bereitstellung für Analytics-, Reporting- und AI-Anwendungsfälle. Dabei arbeitest du in einem interdisziplinären Umfeld an unterschiedlichen Projekten und trägst zur Weiterentwicklung moderner Daten- und AI-Landschaften bei.

  • Verantwortung für Datenlösungen über den gesamten Lebenszyklus hinweg, einschließlich Integration, Betrieb, Weiterentwicklung sowie Orchestrierung, Scheduling und Monitoring (On-Premises und Cloud).
  • Einsatz von GenAI zur Unterstützung von Entwicklung, Dokumentation und Tests sowie Integration von Agentic-AI-Ansätzen unter Berücksichtigung von Sicherheit und Anwendungsnutzen.
  • Konzeption und Umsetzung skalierbarer Datenarchitekturen unter Nutzung gängiger Cloud-Plattformen und Technologien (z. B. Lakehouse, Batch- und Streaming-Verarbeitung, Modern Data Stack).
  • Entwicklung performanter Datenmodelle und Datenpipelines unter Nutzung von SQL sowie Sicherstellung von Datenqualität, Monitoring, Kostenkontrolle und Governance-Anforderungen.
  • Zusammenarbeit mit Kund*innen und interdisziplinären Teams, einschließlich Moderation von Workshops, Erstellung von Prototypen sowie Unterstützung in Angebots- und Umsetzungsphasen.

Dein Profil

  • Erfahrung im Aufbau und Betrieb von Datenplattformen und -pipelines im Projekt- oder Consultingumfeld.
  • Kenntnisse in mindestens einer Cloud- oder Datenplattform (z. B. AWS, Azure, GCP, Databricks, Snowflake) sowie im Modern Data Stack (z. B. dbt, Airflow).
  • Programmierkenntnisse (z. B. Python und SQL) sowie Erfahrung in Datenmodellierung, ETL/ELT- und Streaming-Verfahren, Versionskontrolle (Git) und CI/CD
column3:
Kenntnisse in Datenqualität, Monitoring, Kostenkontrolle sowie Security und Governance.
  • Erfahrung in der Entwicklung und Integration von Datenlösungen einschließlich Tests, Dokumentation sowie der Nutzung moderner AI-Ansätze (z. B. Agentic AI) in Daten-Workflows.
  • Kenntnisse in Anforderungsanalyse, Durchführung von Workshops sowie adressatengerechter Kommunikation
column4:
abgeschlossenes Studium im MINT-Bereich bzw. entsprechende Berufserfahrung sowie sichere Deutsch- und Englischkenntnisse.

4434989296,https://dk.linkedin.com/jobs/view/data-analyst-trust-at-trustpilot-4434989296?position=1&pageNum=0&refId=CfMIyVopItBTcE2aNO1GiA%3D%3D&trackingId=5EqpwCDdhSeyheH8QsMjHA%3D%3D,Data Analyst - Trust,Trustpilot,"Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark","At Trustpilot, we're on an incredible journey. We're a profitable, high-growth FTSE-250 company with a big vision: to become the universal symbol of trust. We run the world's largest open customer review platform, and while we've come a long way, there's still so much exciting work to do. Come join us at the heart of trust!

Trustpilot’s growth has been exponential - from a successful IPO to building out new teams, we never stop working hard to fulfill our mission to create better experiences for everyone through our platform. But to do that, we need data! That’s where Analytics comes in - you’ll help us measure, uncover insights, and predict, to get data everywhere in Trustpilot. We’ve built a world-class analytics team that spans two continents and four cities - are you ready to join a fun, diverse global team?

Trustpilot is on a mission to create better experiences for everyone through our platform. But to do that, we need to protect the integrity of our community and deeply understand how our consumers perceive trust. We’ve built a world-class analytics team that spans continents and cities - and now, we’re looking for a data analyst to support our regulatory duty and protect trust for our platform and consumers.
Trust

This team is the guardian of our platform. They use data to stay one step ahead of bad actors, working with the teams that build the models and detection systems that identify fake reviews and ensure Trustpilot remains a universal symbol of integrity.

It is a huge part of what we do at Trustpilot - it’s in the name! We need to ensure that the content on our platform is real content from real people. That means eliminating scam and spam, and identifying fraudulent behavior. We have several cross functional teams devoted to Trust. You’ll be tightly aligned to their goals, helping teams such as Content Integrity, Legal & Compliance, Corporate Comms & Investor Relations, Fraud & Investigations to investigate requests on fraudulent activity, prepare various reports including core publicly shared company and regulatory reports.

As a Data Analyst at Trustpilot, you will collaborate with Trusties in our Trust, Comms and Marketing teams and spend your time digging through our data to provide them with data-driven insights, as well as analytics and dashboards/reporting. You’re keen to help them use data to answer their toughest questions, and you can proactively identify areas that need a little data support. You’ll be an internal data consultant, with loads of amazing data in our data warehouse at your fingertips, working with analytical tools including BigQuery and Looker.
What you’ll be doing:
Be the main contact point for all regulatory and compliance reporting
Delivering expert analytics and insights to different parts of the business, through both workstream-driven initiatives and ad-hoc inquiries
Building data sets and dashboards that support our Trust data integrity and auditability requirements
Picking the right solution for the problem you’ve got, from your arsenal of analytical tools and methods
Working closely with the Comms, Investor Relations, Marketing, Compliance or Trust teams to understand their data requests and their goals to deliver awesome solutions
Presenting and promoting your work to the wider business, to enable data-informed decision making
Scoping and documenting projects
Standardising and automating key reports ensuring integrity, governance and data quality
Who you are:
Knowledge of reporting best practices and experience delivering actionable insights leveraging BI tools (we use Looker)
Experience in stakeholder and project management, with the confidence to deliver results back to the business
Advanced SQL skills (we use BigQuery)
Data modelling experience: DBT or similar tools used for building data pipelines
Experience with Python is desirable
Familiarity of key analytical and statistical methodologies, such as model-based analyses (regression, clustering etc.), cohort analysis
Familiarity with Airflow
Familiarity with A/B testing
Outstanding written and verbal communication skills
About us

Trustpilot began in 2007 with a simple yet powerful idea that is more relevant today than ever — to be the universal symbol of trust, bringing consumers and businesses together through reviews. Trustpilot is open, independent, and impartial — we help consumers make the right choices and businesses to build trust, grow and improve.

Today, we have more than 350 million reviews and 60 million monthly active users across the globe, with 149 billion annual Trustpilot brand impressions, and the numbers keep growing. We have more than 1000 employees and we’re headquartered in Copenhagen, with operations in  Amsterdam, Denver, Edinburgh, Hamburg, London, Melbourne, Milan and New York.

We’re driven by connection. It’s at the heart of what we do. Our culture keeps things fresh –– it’s built on the relationships we create. We talk, we laugh, we collaborate and we respect each other. We work across borders and cultures to be the universal symbol of trust in an ever-changing world. With vibrant office locations worldwide and over 50 nationalities, we’re proud to be an equal opportunity workplace with diverse perspectives and ideas.

Our purpose to help people and businesses help each other is a tall order, but we keep it real. We’re a great bunch of humans, doing awesome stuff, without fuss or pretense. A successful Trustpilot future is driven by you –– we give you the autonomy to shape a career you can be proud of. If you’re ready to grow, let’s go.
Join us at the heart of trust.

Trustpilot is committed to creating an inclusive environment where people from all backgrounds can thrive and where different viewpoints and experiences are valued and respected. Trustpilot will consider all applications for employment without regard to race, ethnicity, national origin, religious beliefs, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, neurodiversity, disability, age, parental or veteran status. Together, we are the heart of trust.

Trustpilot is a global company and our data practices are designed to ensure that your personally identifiable information is appropriately protected. Please note that your personal information will be transferred, accessed, and stored globally as necessary for the uses and disclosures stated in our Privacy Policy.

If you have a disability and would like to discuss any adjustments you might need either in submitting your application, or to the recruitment process more generally, please let us know by contacting our Talent Acquisition Team (talent.acquisition@trustpilot.com) quoting the role you wish to apply for.

4433383082,https://dk.linkedin.com/jobs/view/data-analyst-at-airbus-4433383082?position=4&pageNum=0&refId=CfMIyVopItBTcE2aNO1GiA%3D%3D&trackingId=URghSJH2i1rxbrvv6fwowg%3D%3D,Data Analyst,Airbus,Copenhagen Metropolitan Area,"Job Description

Would you like to pair practical tasks with data analytics, finding joy in structuring, providing, and explaining data? Do you have a flair for structure and visual presentations? At Satair, data and analytics today serve as one of our largest levers for accelerating a continuous improvement culture, driving efficiency and competitiveness. We are looking for a new colleague with exceptional analytical skills, curiosity for tools and processes and an appetite to help execute on the results derived.

In this role, you will play a key role in enabling data driven decision making to support our mission within Infrastructure & Operations, supporting critical tasks for IT infrastructure and support. Instead of waiting for outcomes to happen, you will help us actively influence our operating terms. You will work within small, multi-disciplinary teams with a core focus on delivering pragmatic, sustainable solutions directly to and with our technical departments. Crucially, you will not be alone or required to build everything from scratch

column1: Tasks & Accountabilities
You will contribute to the team through your analysis and technical execution across the following key responsibilities:

ITSM Platform Transformation: Satair is currently changing its ITSM platform and wants to retain high-level data to monitor developing trends across platforms. In this context, you will handle data transfers and work hand-in-hand with functional teams to develop and improve dashboards and reports.

Actionable Diagnosis & Problem Solving: Ad-hoc tasks will occur where you will be expected to analyze complex technical data to help identify potential root causes. You will deconstruct problems and proactively develop actionable solutions through process automation, tools, dashboards, or presentations.

Pragmatic Execution: You will manage your time dynamically so you do not focus on the analysis only, but also actively commence on execution. This includes developing minimum viable products (MVPs) and digital products that directly address functional pain points.

Community Integration: You will become a part of our wider data mesh community to help us understand and use knowledge gained across the business. Stepping into this well-established data mesh ecosystem means you won't be reinventing the wheel - instead, you will network across teams to re-use existing data products and infrastructure . Within this framework, you will act as a focal point driving awareness and adoption of technologies and analytics.

Stakeholder Management: Your communication skills and structural mindset will be essential, as we serve many different stakeholders and must ensure our messages are well received at all ends. You will articulate complex ideas clearly and effectively to diverse audiences.
Your Profile

We are looking for a quick learner who is passionate about their work, possesses an entrepreneurial mindset, and has a strong drive to get things done. You hold a Bachelor's degree or have 2 - 4 years of experience in a similar position with a strong eagerness to combine deep analysis with practical, hands-on work.
Technical Toolbox
You have a demonstrated understanding of data types and can successfully work with and combine structured and unstructured data.
You possess solid experience with databases, data structures, collaboration tools, and scripting languages.
Robust coding abilities (e.g., Python, SQL) and experience working with APIs, algorithms, or data integrations are considered a highly valuable plus.
Experience with or exposure to Palantir Foundry is highly advantageous, as it serves as a central pillar of our data tech stack and analytics ecosystem
You know how to utilize your own technical ""toolbox"" to share data through structured exhaustive lists, diagrams, or pivot tables.
Mindset & Interpersonal Skills
You have a strong ability to work independently, maintain consistency in terms of deliverables, and bring an independent yet cooperating drive to the team.
You excel at building long-term relationships and networking across a large organization to collaborate, share knowledge, and re-use existing solutions.
You understand the unique challenges of different departments, allowing you to empathize with their concerns and tailor your approach to support technology adoption.

If you want to work as one team embracing a can-do attitude in a demanding environment, then we offer a great place to explore, learn, and grow.
Come on board and join us at Satair, an Airbus Services Company!
About SATAIR

Satair offers first class material management solutions in the aftermarket business of the aviation industry. We are working to be the most customer-centric organization in providing commercial aviation spares parts to make the world continue flying. With our global functional organization spanning across 11 different sites and locations worldwide with more than 1.800 people and a turnover of $3.0 billion, you will be part of a truly global family.

Our customers have the ultimate goal to operate their fleet and our Airbus aircraft safely, efficiently in a cost-time-quality effective manner and of course with the expected comfort level for their customers. We at Satair do everything to support our customers in this.

At SATAIR, you will find much more than a job - we offer you the possibility of growing both your personal and professional capacities in a fun, supportive and informal working environment, where you will be encouraged to engage, share knowledge and ideas.

SATAIR is committed to achieving workforce diversity and creating an inclusive working environment. We love to bring in people with different backgrounds and experiences as we believe it enhances our performance immensely!

Competitive remuneration, including short term incentive bonus on all levels

Personal development: personalized development plans, large portfolio of learning solutions and many internal mobility opportunities, both locally and globally

4432344203,https://dk.linkedin.com/jobs/view/finance-data-analyst-at-lunar-4432344203?position=5&pageNum=0&refId=CfMIyVopItBTcE2aNO1GiA%3D%3D&trackingId=JG3OC6ryAfqnzbNbRMWkCA%3D%3D,Finance Data Analyst,Lunar,"Copenhagen Municipality, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark","Banking hasn't changed much in decades. We're changing that. One product at a time.

Lunar is a Nordic challenger bank building the future of financial life with AI at the core. Our engineers, designers, and product people work on problems that actually matter. Making money simpler, smarter, and more rewarding for a million of registered users across the Nordics.
Fully licensed and founded in 2015, with offices in Copenhagen, Aarhus and Stockholm.
We move fast, we build with purpose, and we're just getting started.

As our new Finance Data Analyst you'll join the Accounting & Control team based in Copenhagen, reporting to Britta Kastler, working to turn messy finance data into something the whole team can trust and build on. To be upfront: the pipes are still being built. You won't inherit a polished warehouse — you'll help shape one, and there's real room to leave your mark.
What will you do?
Partner with the accounting team and other finance teams to automate manual steps in the month-end close, making it faster and less painful every cycle
Help shape the financial data architecture — the foundation everything else in finance is built on
Build and maintain dashboards and reporting — P&L, balance sheet, key KPIs — that finance stakeholders actually rely on
Investigate and resolve data quality issues across finance source systems, and steadily improve data integrity over time

Be the key link between product, tech, and accounting — partnering with the data and tech squads to improve the underlying finance data infrastructure, and translating finance's needs into technical requirements (and back again)
Use AI (Claude) to rethink and overhaul the monthly close, reporting, and other finance data related processes from the ground up
What are we looking for in you?
3–5 years of experience in a data analyst or similar role, with a strong data foundation
Solid hands-on skills in SQL and Python for extracting, transforming, and modelling data
Comfortable working with imperfect, evolving data — you can navigate and improve it rather than waiting for it to be clean
Comfortable around finance concepts and genuinely keen to deepen your understanding (no finance degree required)
Curious about AI and excited to use tools like Claude to make real processes better
What you get.
4 days in the office. We're a team that builds together, thinks together, and moves together. Showing up is part of how we work.
State of the art equipment. The newest computer, monitor, mouse, and keyboard. We want you to do your best work without anything slowing you down.
Pension, health insurance, and enhanced parental leave. From your financial future to your family moments. We've got you covered.
Are you ready to join the journey? Apply now and let's find out more!

While you hold on tight for us to get back to you, curious to see what we’re up to? Follow us on LinkedIn for business announcements and releases 📢, check out our Instagram for an inside scoop on what it’s like to work here .
Depending on the regulations in the country where you will be employed, we will ask to see or obtain information about your criminal record.
We welcome applications from candidates of all backgrounds. If you need any adjustments during the recruitment process, please let us know

4425688315,https://dk.linkedin.com/jobs/view/product-data-analyst-at-n8n-4425688315?position=6&pageNum=0&refId=CfMIyVopItBTcE2aNO1GiA%3D%3D&trackingId=ZS126vjIMLPKoV2iFV7n%2Bg%3D%3D,Product Data Analyst,n8n,Denmark,"The AI orchestration of your wildest imagination.

n8n is the open workflow orchestration platform built for the new era of AI. We give technical teams the freedom of code with the speed of no-code, so they can automate faster, smarter, and without limits. Backed by a fiercely inventive community and 500+ builder-approved integrations, we’re changing the way people bring systems together and scale ideas for impact.

Since our founding in 2019, we’ve grown into a diverse team of over 260 - working across Europe and the US, connected by a shared builder spirit and with our centre of gravity in Berlin. Along the way, we’ve:
Cultivated a community of more than 650,000 active developers and builders
Earned 190K+ GitHub stars, making us one of the world’s Top 40 most popular projects
Backed by top investors, from Sequoia’s first German seed to our SAP's recent strategic investment - bringing us to a $5.2bn valuation

That’s the company we’ve built. Now we’d love to see what you can build. If you’re applying, try n8n out - whether you’re technical or not - and share a screenshot of your first workflow with us. The easiest place to start is here: app.n8n.cloud/register.
We’re in a defining moment of an incredible journey. Come and build with us.
Join n8n as the dedicated data partner to a product team that's already hands-on with data.

Our PMs are technical and data-literate. They write SQL, run their own analyses, and know their corner of the product deeply. What they're missing is a dedicated analyst counterpart - someone who takes the data work further than they have time for, brings the outside perspective they can't get from inside their squad, and pairs with them on designing and improving the product.

That's the role. You'll be embedded with a product squad as the PMs' dedicated data partner, working on the deeper analyses, the cross-cutting views, and the questions that shape what gets built - with activation and adoption of n8n as your headline focus.
🎯 Your mission: be the dedicated data partner that helps a hands-on product team go deeper, see further, and design better products.
🤝 Be the PMs' data partner
Our PMs already do their own analyses. You're the counterpart who takes the work deeper and broader than they can in a sprint.
Take on the analyses they don't have time for, accelerate the ones they're running, and pair on the questions that matter most.
Free PMs to focus on building product by being the dedicated owner of the data work that supports it.
🔭 Bring perspective they can't get themselves
Cross-product cuts, segment views, comparative analyses across the user base — the outside lens that's hard to see from inside a single squad.
Challenge assumptions, including the ones PMs are most attached to.
Surface the analyses nobody asked for but everyone needed.
🚀 Help design and improve the product
Co-create hypotheses with PMs and pair on how to test them properly.
Activation and adoption are the headline: how users get to value, what drives lasting use, where we're losing them, and what to build to fix it.
Go deep on retention drivers, cohort dynamics, and the leading indicators of expansion. Connect feature-level analyses back to the bigger picture.
🗣 Influence the roadmap with a real POV
Sit in product planning as a peer. Bring strong, data-backed opinions about what we should build next — and what we shouldn't.
Push back constructively. Translate complex analysis into crisp recommendations that move decisions.
🧪 Raise the bar on rigor
Bring statistical depth where it matters and keep it out of the way when it doesn't.
Make experiments and analyses trustworthy — well-designed, properly powered, honestly read.
Partner with PMs and Engineering on event instrumentation so the data the team relies on is solid.
Requirements
Must-haves
📊 Senior product analytics chops: 5+ years analyzing product usage in B2B SaaS, ideally in a PLG environment.
🤝 Partner mindset: You thrive working alongside technical, hands-on PMs as a counterpart — making them better and being made better by them.
🚀 Activation & adoption expertise: You've owned the analysis behind onboarding funnels, time-to-value, feature adoption, and the behavioral signals that separate stuck users from successful ones.
🔎 Depth, not just breadth: You're known for going deeper than the obvious — behavioral segmentation, retention drivers, feature impact done properly.
🤓 Real applied statistics: Not ""I took a stats class"" — you can spot a biased metric, an underpowered test, or a confounder, and explain it clearly to a non-technical stakeholder.
🧭 Opinionated and high-agency: You don't wait for tickets. You see what's worth analyzing, you do it, and you bring a clear recommendation — even when the team didn't ask.
🧪 Solid experimentation skills: You can design and read A/B tests properly. It's one tool in your kit, not the only one.
🔧 Instrumentation fluency: You've defined event taxonomies and partnered with engineering on tracking specs.
💻 Technical toolkit: Expert SQL
column2: comfort with modern stack (BigQuery, dbt, Metabase or equivalent).

column1: 🗣️ Communication that lands: You write memos PMs read end-to-end and present findings that change decisions.
Nice-to-haves
👾 Devtool / OSS / technical product experience: You get how technical audiences adopt and use products.
🐍 Python for analysis: pandas, statsmodels, scipy when SQL isn't enough.
🔗 Causal inference: For the questions A/B testing can't answer.
🧮 Experimentation platforms: GrowthBook, Statsig, Eppo, or similar.
Why join us?

At n8n, your work won't disappear into a slide deck. You'll pair with a technical, data-literate product team that already does its own analysis and genuinely values having a real analyst counterpart. You'll be embedded, trusted, and in the room where decisions get made — owning the questions that matter most for a fast-growing PLG product: how users activate, what drives adoption, and what keeps them coming back. You'll join a small, sharp data team with strong foundations already in place, inside one of Europe's fastest-growing open-source companies, at a moment where what we ship matters a lot.
Sound like a challenge you're ready to take on?
Apply now - and help us build the future of automation.

Benefits
Competitive compensation 💸 – We offer fair and attractive pay.
Ownership 💪 – Our core value is to “empower others,” and we mean it—you’ll get a slice of n8n with equity.
Work/life balance 🏖️ – We work hard but ensure you have time to recharge:
Europe: 30 days of vacation, plus public holidays wherever you are.
US: 20 vacation days, 8 sick days, plus public holidays wherever you are.
Health & wellness 🩺 –
Europe: We provide benefits according to local country norms.*
US: Multiple low-premium, low-deductible medical plans with coverage for individuals and families—plus a no-cost premium HDHP option with a pre-seeded HSA—along with dental and vision coverage.
Future planning 💰 –
Europe: We provide pension contributions according to local country norms.*
US: 401(k) retirement plan with a 4% employer match.
Financial security 🛡️ –
Europe: We provide benefits according to local country norms.*
US: Company-paid short-term and long-term disability insurance, plus life insurance to support you and your loved ones.
Career growth 📈 – We hire rising stars who grow with us! You’ll get €1K (or equivalent) per year to spend on courses, books, events, or coaching to level up your skills.
A passionate team 🤩 – We love our product, and we prove it with regular hackathons where we see who can build the coolest thing with it!
Remote-first 🌏 – Our team works remotely across Europe, with regular off-sites for team bonding. Some roles, like sales in the US, are hybrid—please check the job description.

The AI orchestration of your wildest imagination.

n8n is the open workflow orchestration platform built for the new era of AI. We give technical teams the freedom of code with the speed of no-code, so they can automate faster, smarter, and without limits. Backed by a fiercely inventive community and 500+ builder-approved integrations, we’re changing the way people bring systems together and scale ideas for impact.

Since our founding in 2019, we’ve grown into a diverse team of over 260 - working across Europe and the US, connected by a shared builder spirit and with our centre of gravity in Berlin. Along the way, we’ve:

  • Cultivated a community of more than 650,000 active developers and builders
  • Earned 190K+ GitHub stars, making us one of the world’s Top 40 most popular projects
  • Backed by top investors, from Sequoia’s first German seed to our SAP's recent strategic investment - bringing us to a $5.2bn valuation

That’s the company we’ve built. Now we’d love to see what you can build. If you’re applying, try n8n out - whether you’re technical or not - and share a screenshot of your first workflow with us. The easiest place to start is here: app.n8n.cloud/register.

We’re in a defining moment of an incredible journey. Come and build with us.

Join n8n as the dedicated data partner to a product team that's already hands-on with data.

Our PMs are technical and data-literate. They write SQL, run their own analyses, and know their corner of the product deeply. What they're missing is a dedicated analyst counterpart - someone who takes the data work further than they have time for, brings the outside perspective they can't get from inside their squad, and pairs with them on designing and improving the product.

That's the role. You'll be embedded with a product squad as the PMs' dedicated data partner, working on the deeper analyses, the cross-cutting views, and the questions that shape what gets built - with activation and adoption of n8n as your headline focus.

🎯 Your mission: be the dedicated data partner that helps a hands-on product team go deeper, see further, and design better products.

🤝 Be the PMs' data partner

  • Our PMs already do their own analyses. You're the counterpart who takes the work deeper and broader than they can in a sprint.
  • Take on the analyses they don't have time for, accelerate the ones they're running, and pair on the questions that matter most.
  • Free PMs to focus on building product by being the dedicated owner of the data work that supports it.

🔭 Bring perspective they can't get themselves

  • Cross-product cuts, segment views, comparative analyses across the user base — the outside lens that's hard to see from inside a single squad.
  • Challenge assumptions, including the ones PMs are most attached to.
  • Surface the analyses nobody asked for but everyone needed.

🚀 Help design and improve the product

  • Co-create hypotheses with PMs and pair on how to test them properly.
  • Activation and adoption are the headline: how users get to value, what drives lasting use, where we're losing them, and what to build to fix it.
  • Go deep on retention drivers, cohort dynamics, and the leading indicators of expansion. Connect feature-level analyses back to the bigger picture.

🗣 Influence the roadmap with a real POV

  • Sit in product planning as a peer. Bring strong, data-backed opinions about what we should build next — and what we shouldn't.
  • Push back constructively. Translate complex analysis into crisp recommendations that move decisions.

🧪 Raise the bar on rigor

  • Bring statistical depth where it matters and keep it out of the way when it doesn't.
  • Make experiments and analyses trustworthy — well-designed, properly powered, honestly read.
  • Partner with PMs and Engineering on event instrumentation so the data the team relies on is solid.

Requirements

Must-haves

  • 📊 Senior product analytics chops: 5+ years analyzing product usage in B2B SaaS, ideally in a PLG environment.
  • 🤝 Partner mindset: You thrive working alongside technical, hands-on PMs as a counterpart — making them better and being made better by them.
  • 🚀 Activation &
column2:
adoption expertise: You've owned the analysis behind onboarding funnels, time-to-value, feature adoption, and the behavioral signals that separate stuck users from successful ones.
  • 🔎 Depth, not just breadth: You're known for going deeper than the obvious — behavioral segmentation, retention drivers, feature impact done properly.
  • 🤓 Real applied statistics: Not ""I took a stats class"" — you can spot a biased metric, an underpowered test, or a confounder, and explain it clearly to a non-technical stakeholder.
  • 🧭 Opinionated and high-agency: You don't wait for tickets. You see what's worth analyzing, you do it, and you bring a clear recommendation — even when the team didn't ask.
  • 🧪 Solid experimentation skills: You can design and read A/B tests properly. It's one tool in your kit, not the only one.
  • 🔧 Instrumentation fluency: You've defined event taxonomies and partnered with engineering on tracking specs.
  • 💻 Technical toolkit: Expert SQL
column3:
comfort with modern stack (BigQuery, dbt, Metabase or equivalent).
  • 🗣️ Communication that lands: You write memos PMs read end-to-end and present findings that change decisions.

    • Nice-to-haves

    • 👾 Devtool / OSS / technical product experience: You get how technical audiences adopt and use products.
    • 🐍 Python for analysis: pandas, statsmodels, scipy when SQL isn't enough.
    • 🔗 Causal inference: For the questions A/B testing can't answer.
    • 🧮 Experimentation platforms: GrowthBook, Statsig, Eppo, or similar.

    Why join us?

    At n8n, your work won't disappear into a slide deck. You'll pair with a technical, data-literate product team that already does its own analysis and genuinely values having a real analyst counterpart. You'll be embedded, trusted, and in the room where decisions get made — owning the questions that matter most for a fast-growing PLG product: how users activate, what drives adoption, and what keeps them coming back. You'll join a small, sharp data team with strong foundations already in place, inside one of Europe's fastest-growing open-source companies, at a moment where what we ship matters a lot.

    Sound like a challenge you're ready to take on?

    Apply now - and help us build the future of automation.

4432338644,https://dk.linkedin.com/jobs/view/finance-data-analyst-at-lunar-4432338644?position=9&pageNum=0&refId=CfMIyVopItBTcE2aNO1GiA%3D%3D&trackingId=7J4blzDR%2B1NMiHbdj8m3IA%3D%3D,Finance Data Analyst,Lunar,"Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark","Banking hasn't changed much in decades. We're changing that. One product at a time.

Lunar is a Nordic challenger bank building the future of financial life with AI at the core. Our engineers, designers, and product people work on problems that actually matter. Making money simpler, smarter, and more rewarding for a million of registered users across the Nordics.
Fully licensed and founded in 2015, with offices in Copenhagen, Aarhus and Stockholm.
We move fast, we build with purpose, and we're just getting started.

As our new Finance Data Analyst you'll join the Accounting & Control team based in Copenhagen, reporting to Britta Kastler, working to turn messy finance data into something the whole team can trust and build on. To be upfront: the pipes are still being built. You won't inherit a polished warehouse — you'll help shape one, and there's real room to leave your mark.
What will you do?
Partner with the accounting team and other finance teams to automate manual steps in the month-end close, making it faster and less painful every cycle.
Help shape the financial data architecture — the foundation everything else in finance is built on.
Build and maintain dashboards and reporting — P&L, balance sheet, key KPIs — that finance stakeholders actually rely on.
Investigate and resolve data quality issues across finance source systems, and steadily improve data integrity over time.

Be the key link between product, tech, and accounting — partnering with the data and tech squads to improve the underlying finance data infrastructure, and translating finance's needs into technical requirements (and back again).
Use AI (Claude) to rethink and overhaul the monthly close, reporting, and other finance data related processes from the ground up.
What are we looking for in you?
3–5 years of experience in a data analyst or similar role, with a strong data foundation.
Solid hands-on skills in SQL and Python for extracting, transforming, and modelling data.
Comfortable working with imperfect, evolving data — you can navigate and improve it rather than waiting for it to be clean.
Comfortable around finance concepts and genuinely keen to deepen your understanding (no finance degree required).
Curious about AI and excited to use tools like Claude to make real processes better.
What you get.
4 days in the office. We're a team that builds together, thinks together, and moves together. Showing up is part of how we work.
State of the art equipment. The newest computer, monitor, mouse, and keyboard. We want you to do your best work without anything slowing you down.
Pension, health insurance, and enhanced parental leave. From your financial future to your family moments. We've got you covered.
Are you ready to join the journey? Apply now and let's find out more!

While you hold on tight for us to get back to you, curious to see what we’re up to? Follow us on LinkedIn for business announcements and releases 📢, check out our Instagram for an inside scoop on what it’s like to work here .
Depending on the regulations in the country where you will be employed, we will ask to see or obtain information about your criminal record.
We welcome applications from candidates of all backgrounds. If you need any adjustments during the recruitment process, please let us know

4426362394,https://dk.linkedin.com/jobs/view/data-analyst-at-skatteguiden-4426362394?position=10&pageNum=0&refId=CfMIyVopItBTcE2aNO1GiA%3D%3D&trackingId=7YRMyTtAogtldpVCxE5ggw%3D%3D,Data Analyst,Skatteguiden,"Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark","Tech-first. Ego-free. User-obsessed. Over én million danskere bruger Skatteguiden til at forstå og optimere deres skat. Vi automatiserer skatteovervågning, finder fradrag, som folk ikke vidste de havde, og giver brugerne indsigt til bedre økonomiske beslutninger — direkte forbundet til SKAT. Vi er ~50 mennesker i København, med en 4,7-stjernet rating og én mission: gør finansiel indsigt tilgængelig for alle, ikke kun dem der har råd til en revisor.
Rollen:

Vi har brug for en analytiker, der kan gå i dybden med den skattedata, der driver vores produkter. Du bliver den i datateamet, der forstår skattedata bedst — og en central sparringspartner for de produktteams, der bygger vores skatteberegnings- og fradragsprodukter. Du behøver ikke kende dansk skattelovgivning fra dag ét. Du skal være skarp nok på data til at lære det hurtigt.
Hvad du kommer til at arbejde med
Modellere og dokumentere skattedata i dbt på Snowflake
Udvikle og validere prædiktive modeller — dokumenteret klar til engineering handover
Designe og køre simuleringer med live data og konstruerede scenarier
Vedligeholde skattedatamodeller i takt med regulatoriske ændringer
Bidrage til observability og datakvalitet med et datadrevet perspektiv
Hvad du bringer
Stærkt SQL-fundament og erfaring med kompleks datamodellering
Erfaring med vores data stack: Snowflake, dbt, Lightdash, Evidence eller tilsvarende tooling.
Solid erfaring med prædiktiv modellering og modelvalidering
Analytisk skarp med øje for edge cases og korrekthed
Trives i et tværfunktionelt produktteam — ikke en ren engineering-rolle
Erfaring med Lightdash. Python.
Godt at have kendskab til dansk skattelovgivning, finans eller revision.
Taler flydende dansk
Hvad vi tilbyder
En teknisk dyb rolle i et komplekst og meningsfuldt domæne
Muligheden for at blive organisationens foretrukne ekspert på skattedata
Et stærkt data team med høje faglige standarder og moderne tech stack
Office first i København — fem min fra Rådhuspladsen, frokost hos Claus Meyer, aftensmad hvis dagen er lang
Praktisk Start: Hurtigst muligt
Rapporterer til: Head of Data & Operations Planning
Løn: Kompetitiv og erfaringsbaseret
Our Lean Hiring process
Screening call med Christian Payne, 30 min (online)
45 minutters møde med hiring manager og product manager (onsite)
1 times case session med hiring manager og teammedlem, efterfulgt af kort snak med Nikolai (CEO), 30 min (onsite)

4432667016,https://dk.linkedin.com/jobs/view/business-intelligence-specialist-at-boozt-4432667016?position=15&pageNum=0&refId=CfMIyVopItBTcE2aNO1GiA%3D%3D&trackingId=%2BWUTgVnvi9C6JW7HAwwJlQ%3D%3D,Business Intelligence Specialist,Boozt,"Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark","THE OPPORTUNITY
As the leading Nordic online department store - 1,600+ brands, 14 markets, one of the world's most automated fulfilment centres - data powers every decision we make at Boozt.
We're hiring two new colleagues across our BI function: one in our Logistics Reporting & Analytics (Warehouse & operations) and in our Commercial Business Partner Team.
Different teams, different challenges, one thing in common: your data insights help shape smarter decisions and turn them into real business impact.
Logistics Reporting & Analytics: Early Career
Operations-focused

Learn the business from the ground up. Every order that reaches a customer is the result of an efficient supply chain, a distribution network, and a customer service operation working in sync. You'll sit close to the warehouse floor, fulfilment, returns, and last mile, turning operational data into dashboards and analyses that help teams move faster and smarter.
WHAT YOU WILL DO
Build, develop, and maintain QlikCloud apps end to end - from data load and modelling to the dashboards teams use every day
Write and optimise load scripts, build data models, and keep apps fast and reliable as data volumes grow
Run ad hoc analysis in Qlik to answer operational questions quickly when they come up
Turn requirements from distribution, supply chain, and customer service teams into well-built, reusable reporting
Maintain and improve our existing Qlik reporting - refactoring, fixing, and extending what's already there
Work with our data and engineering teams on data quality and the pipelines that feed Qlik (BigQuery)
YOUR PROFILE
2> years in a BI, analytics data role - internships, thesis work, and student jobs count
Comfortable with SQL and Qlik scripting / data modelling, or genuinely motivated to get there fast
Hands-on with QlikCloud, or able to learn it quickly (experience with Power BI, Tableau, or Looker transfers well
column2: we use Qlik with BigQuery)

column1: You move from ""here's the data"" to ""here's what we should build""
Curious about how the business actually works, not just the numbers it produces
Commercial Business Partner Team: Mid-level
Engineering-focused

Work with large and complex datasets to build the data foundation behind key commercial decisions. You'll design and maintain the data models, pipelines, and BigQuery architecture that our commercial teams rely on to understand performance, set priorities, and identify growth opportunities. Your work powers high-impact commercial decisions.
WHAT YOU WILL DO
Design and maintain the BigQuery data infrastructure powering commercial reporting
Develop Qlik Sense scripts and maintain dashboards and lead our migration to Qlik Cloud
Integrate data from multiple source systems using SQL, including one-off data pulls when needed
Document data models, calculations, and mappings so the work stays transparent and maintainable
Partner with analysts and business partners to turn business requirements into robust data products
YOUR PROFILE
2+< years building BI solutions end to end: data models, pipelines, KPIs, dashboards
Strong SQL and scripting skills, with hands-on experience integrating data from multiple source systems
Proactive and driving, you push work forward without waiting to be told
Comfortable translating non-technical requirements into technical solutions
Bachelor's in computer science, information systems, or a related field
column2: fluent English

Great personal and internal career development opportunities
A culture built on our values of trust, freedom, and responsibility
Driven and passionate international colleagues
A generous employee discount
Healthy and delicious breakfast and lunch, top-quality coffee, and fresh fruit available all day
In-house gym, onsite masseuse, and medical doctor
An amazing HQ in central Copenhagen – the iconic, energy-efficient Krystallen building, recognized for its architectural design and sustainability. Enjoy breathtaking city views and much more!

4434480094,https://dk.linkedin.com/jobs/view/data-engineer-at-snipr-a-crispr-company-4434480094?position=19&pageNum=0&refId=CfMIyVopItBTcE2aNO1GiA%3D%3D&trackingId=nZ3r%2F2dbPuJOOjX%2BFk%2BFbg%3D%3D,Data Engineer,SNIPR - A CRISPR Company,"Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark","Data Engineer
SNIPR | Copenhagen Ø, Denmark | Full-time | 2-year temporary position with the possibility of extension or permanent employment
Are you a data engineer who wants your pipelines, databases, and applications to power real-world medicine of tomorrow - the kind that could change how the world fights antibiotic resistance?

At SNIPR we are developing a new generation of medicines that use CRISPR to target harmful bacteria. Behind that science sits a growing amount of data, and we are looking for a data engineer to build and maintain the infrastructure that keeps it reliable, connected, and trustworthy. It is a broad, hands-on role with real room to shape how we work and you would be the person who owns and develops this area.
You will join a diverse, international team of 40+ in the heart of Copenhagen’s Innovation District (www.sniprbiome.com).
ABOUT SNIPR

SNIPR is a Copenhagen-based clinical stage biotech company developing a new kind of medicine to fight one of the biggest threats to global health: bacteria that no longer respond to classic antibiotics. Using CRISPR, a technology that can be programmed to recognise specific DNA, we are building therapies that target harmful, drug-resistant bacteria while leaving the body's healthy bacteria untouched.

Our lead treatment is already in Phase 2 clinical trials, with results from our development work and first human study published in Nature Biotechnology and The Lancet Microbe. Our work is supported by partners including CARB-X, the Gates Foundation, the Cystic Fibrosis Foundation, and the European Investment Bank.
We have raised more than EUR 90 million, hold 30+ granted US patents, and bring together a diverse team of scientists, engineers, and specialists from leading institutions and companies worldwide.
THE ROLE

As Data Engineer, you will build and maintain the pipelines, databases, and web applications that connect our lab instruments, external data warehouses, and laboratory information management system (LIMS) into one coherent, reliable data infrastructure. The researchers and data scientists you support depend on this data being available, clean, and trustworthy, and your work makes that possible.
This is a broad and open role with genuine scope to develop the area. The position is primarily data engineering, with some of your time supporting our IT and digital infrastructure function.
You will report to the Director of Bioinformatics and work closely with both lab scientists and our bioinformatics and data science colleagues. The role is based on site at our Copenhagen offices.
KEY RESPONSIBILITIES
Build and maintain production web applications and the data pipelines behind them (for example in Python/Django).
Design, manage, and integrate structured/relational databases, our LIMS, and external scientific platforms via APIs.
Manage and deploy infrastructure on cloud platforms (e.g. AWS): Including compute, storage, networking, and access control.
Ensure reliability, security, and clear documentation across the data infrastructure.
Work closely with lab scientists and data scientists to translate research needs into practical technical solutions.
IT & infrastructure support (secondary part of the role)
Alongside the data engineering work, you will lend a hand to our IT and digital infrastructure function. Experience here is welcome but not essential — this is an area you can grow into:
Network management (switches, routers, access points) and NAS storage.
Cybersecurity good practice across cloud, API, and network layers.
Microsoft Intune / M365 administration.
QUALIFICATIONS
Required
Around 2+ years of hands-on experience as a data engineer, including implementing and running solutions in a production environment.
Strong experience building and maintaining production web applications (for example with Python/Django).
Solid SQL and structured/relational database experience.
Experience managing and deploying infrastructure on cloud platforms (e.g. AWS), including compute, storage, networking, and access control.
Strong troubleshooting skills and a structured approach to documentation, reliability, and security.
Version control (Git) and CI/CD practices.
Nice to have
Experience integrating scientific platforms such as Benchling, a LIMS, or similar.
Experience in life sciences or biotech environments.
Experience handling next-generation sequencing data, including storage, transfer, organisation, and analysis.
Systems programming in a low-level language (e.g. Rust or C/C++).
Familiarity with IT/infrastructure support (networking, cybersecurity, Microsoft Intune / M365).
You are
Curious and eager to learn, with the drive to take ownership of an area and develop it.
A strong collaborator who works well across technical and scientific teams.
Structured, reliable, and quality-focused.
Motivated by the idea of building infrastructure that supports meaningful science.
WHAT WE OFFER
A broad, hands-on role with real ownership and the freedom to shape SNIPR's data infrastructure.
A 2-year temporary position with the possibility of extension or permanent employment, in a fast-moving, clinical-stage biotech working at the forefront of CRISPR medicine.
Competitive salary and a comprehensive benefits package including health insurance, pension, and bonus.
A diverse, international, collegial environment in the heart of Copenhagen's Innovation District.
HOW TO APPLY
Please submit your application in English, including your CV with a short motivating paragraph, combined in a single document.
We will review applications on a rolling basis, and online interviews may be offered in the initial stages of the process.
Application deadline: 2 August 2026

SNIPR | Copenhagen Ø, Denmark | Full-time | 2-year temporary position with the possibility of extension or permanent employment


Are you a data engineer who wants your pipelines, databases, and applications to power real-world medicine of tomorrow - the kind that could change how the world fights antibiotic resistance?

At SNIPR we are developing a new generation of medicines that use CRISPR to target harmful bacteria. Behind that science sits a growing amount of data, and we are looking for a data engineer to build and maintain the infrastructure that keeps it reliable, connected, and trustworthy. It is a broad, hands-on role with real room to shape how we work and you would be the person who owns and develops this area.

You will join a diverse, international team of 40+ in the heart of Copenhagen’s Innovation District (www.sniprbiome.com).


ABOUT SNIPR

SNIPR is a Copenhagen-based clinical stage biotech company developing a new kind of medicine to fight one of the biggest threats to global health: bacteria that no longer respond to classic antibiotics. Using CRISPR, a technology that can be programmed to recognise specific DNA, we are building therapies that target harmful, drug-resistant bacteria while leaving the body's healthy bacteria untouched.

Our lead treatment is already in Phase 2 clinical trials, with results from our development work and first human study published in Nature Biotechnology and The Lancet Microbe. Our work is supported by partners including CARB-X, the Gates Foundation, the Cystic Fibrosis Foundation, and the European Investment Bank.

We have raised more than EUR 90 million, hold 30+ granted US patents, and bring together a diverse team of scientists, engineers, and specialists from leading institutions and companies worldwide.


THE ROLE

As Data Engineer, you will build and maintain the pipelines, databases, and web applications that connect our lab instruments, external data warehouses, and laboratory information management system (LIMS) into one coherent, reliable data infrastructure. The researchers and data scientists you support depend on this data being available, clean, and trustworthy, and your work makes that possible.

This is a broad and open role with genuine scope to develop the area. The position is primarily data engineering, with some of your time supporting our IT and digital infrastructure function.

You will report to the Director of Bioinformatics and work closely with both lab scientists and our bioinformatics and data science colleagues. The role is based on site at our Copenhagen offices.


KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

  • Build and maintain production web applications and the data pipelines behind them (for example in Python/Django).
  • Design, manage, and integrate structured/relational databases, our LIMS, and external scientific platforms via APIs.
  • Manage and deploy infrastructure on cloud platforms (e.g. AWS): Including compute, storage, networking, and access control.
  • Ensure reliability, security, and clear documentation across the data infrastructure.
  • Work closely with lab scientists and data scientists to translate research needs into practical technical solutions.


IT &
column2:
infrastructure support (secondary part of the role)

Alongside the data engineering work, you will lend a hand to our IT and digital infrastructure function. Experience here is welcome but not essential — this is an area you can grow into:

  • Network management (switches, routers, access points) and NAS storage.
  • Cybersecurity good practice across cloud, API, and network layers.
  • Microsoft Intune / M365 administration.


QUALIFICATIONS

Required

  • Around 2+ years of hands-on experience as a data engineer, including implementing and running solutions in a production environment.
  • Strong experience building and maintaining production web applications (for example with Python/Django).
  • Solid SQL and structured/relational database experience.
  • Experience managing and deploying infrastructure on cloud platforms (e.g. AWS), including compute, storage, networking, and access control.
  • Strong troubleshooting skills and a structured approach to documentation, reliability, and security.
  • Version control (Git) and CI/CD practices.


Nice to have

  • Experience integrating scientific platforms such as Benchling, a LIMS, or similar.
  • Experience in life sciences or biotech environments.
  • Experience handling next-generation sequencing data, including storage, transfer, organisation, and analysis.
  • Systems programming in a low-level language (e.g. Rust or C/C++).
  • Familiarity with IT/infrastructure support (networking, cybersecurity, Microsoft Intune / M365).


You are

  • Curious and eager to learn, with the drive to take ownership of an area and develop it.
  • A strong collaborator who works well across technical and scientific teams.
  • Structured, reliable, and quality-focused.
  • Motivated by the idea of building infrastructure that supports meaningful science.


WHAT WE OFFER

  • A broad, hands-on role with real ownership and the freedom to shape SNIPR's data infrastructure.
  • A 2-year temporary position with the possibility of extension or permanent employment, in a fast-moving, clinical-stage biotech working at the forefront of CRISPR medicine.
  • Competitive salary and a comprehensive benefits package including health insurance, pension, and bonus.
  • A diverse, international, collegial environment in the heart of Copenhagen's Innovation District.


HOW TO APPLY

Please submit your application in English, including your CV with a short motivating paragraph, combined in a single document.

We will review applications on a rolling basis, and online interviews may be offered in the initial stages of the process.


Application deadline: 2 August 2026

In your Business Intelligence or data science role, you will be working with various solutions that support Systematic’s business areas.
This involves the development of products and solutions for our customers and other business units – all in close collaboration with highly skilled colleagues.
Some of the tasks you will be working with involve:
Identifying and understanding the needs of both the customer and the product organisation through close dialogue
Using data to create value for our customers
Developing, coding and implementing BI/data science solutions
Cleaning and analysing data, and implementing and developing machine learning models
Facilitating workshops and building data models that support domains, user behaviour and software

You can expect to work with a range of technologies, such as Microsoft Power BI, Python, Azure Cloud, Microsoft SQL Server, T-SQL, Microsoft SSIS and SSAS, data warehousing, data modelling and analysis.

We enjoy a very high level of job satisfaction, and the dynamics between experienced experts and young talents contribute to a high level of development and progress.
We work closely together across the different disciplines and areas of expertise, with teams comprising project managers, consultants, data scientists, software architects and business developers.

A key characteristic of the teams at Systematic is that we dare to challenge and develop ourselves and each other. We focus on knowledge-sharing in our work, and always find more innovative ways of doing things.
You understand the importance of BI and data science
You are creative, good at spotting opportunities and think out of the box. You have a solid understanding of the interplay between people, technologies and processes.
You have strong communication skills combined with sound business acumen, and you enjoy being involved in a wide variety of assignments.
You stay calm when faced with numerous tasks and requirements, and you maintain an overview and understand how to prioritise the tasks at every stage of the project.
You are organised, analytical and you are driven by the quality of your work. You enjoy presenting new ideas and have what it takes to garner the support of your colleagues and business partners.
At the same time, you are a team player who enjoys the prospect of actively contributing to the team and participating in development activities.
You are fluent in English, both orally and in writing. It is an advantage if you speak Danish, but this is not a requirement.

4390312366,https://dk.linkedin.com/jobs/view/senior-data-analyst-relocate-to-cyprus-at-scorewarrior-4390312366?position=24&pageNum=0&refId=CfMIyVopItBTcE2aNO1GiA%3D%3D&trackingId=4lCgKG6vHY3piaQqoKVdEw%3D%3D,Senior Data Analyst (Relocate to Cyprus),Scorewarrior,"Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark","We are looking for an experienced Data Analyst to join our Player Care team at Scorewarrior. The Player Care team is at the forefront of providing customer service to our players, as well as safeguarding the community integrity through enforcing Fair & Safe Play, improving moderation efforts, and ensuring operational excellence while at it.

Scorewarrior is a game developer and publisher based in Limassol, Cyprus. The company focuses on the 4X strategy genre, with an ambitious goal to grow its hit game, Total Battle, to become the #1 strategy game in the world, and this role is key to achieving this. Our team consists of 200+ passionate, talented, self-driven, and continually developing professionals who love making high-quality games.

Our product: We are currently working on Total Battle — an MMO 4X strategy game with a deep political system and lots of player interaction. Players take on the role of a commander and need to develop their city, forge alliances, fight against monsters, and conquer other players. We're constantly developing new game mechanics, and the project is continuing to grow.
At the heart of our Player Care department, our mission is to excite our players with exceptional customer care, ensuring they feel valued and supported throughout their journey with us.
What you’ll do
Build and maintain the single ""Source of Truth"" semantic data layer for the entire Player Care department, enabling non-technical leads to perform ad-hoc analysis.
Design, build, and maintain reliable metrics dashboards tailored to the operational needs of different Support and Trust & Safety teams.
Ensure all operational metrics (CSAT, SLA, etc.) are clearly defined, reliable, and calculated correctly across all reports.
Assist and mentor the support team with SQL query composition and data literacy.
Conduct A/B tests to measure the effect of Support and Trust & Safety initiatives on relevant departmental and broader business metrics.
Analyze the correlation between support quality, bug-related friction, and subsequent player behavior, with a strong focus on VIP cohorts.
Partner with the safety team to analyze data for fraud detection, including social engineering and account sharing.
Perform time-series forecasting for workload volume based on the LiveOps calendar to optimize shift headcount and SLA coverage.
As a future ambition, build and maintain an early warning predictive model for VIP player churn.
What you have
Strong SQL skills and data modeling experience
Experience with BI tools (e.g. Tableau or equivalents)
Proven experience in product or operational analytics
Ability to design analytical events and metrics aligned with product goals
Strong analytical thinking and ability to translate data into actionable insights
Confident knowledge of mathematical statistics and probability theory
Hands-on experience with causal inference and A/B testing
Familiarity and advocacy of AI tooling (ideally Claude)
Patience and willingness to advocate for data literacy and help operational teams learn basic data concepts
Nice to haves
Hands-on experience with ClickHouse and dbt
Experience analyzing LiveOps features, complex game economies, or VIP player behavior, ideally in mid-core or 4x strategy games.
Experience working with Customer Support or Trust & Safety (fraud detection, toxicity)
Experience working with large player bases and complex event-driven systems
Where you’ll work

We value face-to-face communication, and we strongly believe that genuine human interactions move us closer to our ambitious goal. This is part of our culture, and this is why this job offers no remote or hybrid work opportunities.
You will be working in Limassol, Cyprus. If you’re not from around here, fear not! Our dedicated relocation team will provide comprehensive support helping you and your family move to Limassol.
What we offer
The best team in the world!
Salary of 5000-7000 eur net/month + 13th salary.
Fully-covered relocation to Limassol, Cyprus for you and your family.
A modern and sunny office space close to the sea.
Private health insurance for you and your family members.
A monthly school allowance for your children at pre-school and school-age.
Lunch compensation.
Personal development opportunities paid for by the company: professional and language courses, books, etc.
Bonuses for outstanding results, years worked, getting married, and the birth of a child.
Monthly office parties and epic, annual offsite company birthday and Christmas parties.

4434477383,https://dk.linkedin.com/jobs/view/data-engineer-at-cvation-part-of-skaylink-4434477383?position=25&pageNum=0&refId=CfMIyVopItBTcE2aNO1GiA%3D%3D&trackingId=buqZUgQ4Kms8TT1VKdhTeA%3D%3D,Data Engineer,cVation - Part of Skaylink,"Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark","Azure Data Engineer

Som Data Engineer hos cVation bygger du innovative dataplatforme og datawarehouses i Azure. Du arbejder med ETL-processer og automatisering af dataflows, og du bygger skalerbare, driftssikre løsninger for vores kunder. Vi bruger teknologier som Fabric, Databricks, Spark, Azure Data Factory, Terraform, Git og CI/CD. Fokus er på data engineering, pipelines og cloud-infrastruktur.

cVation er et dansk udviklings- og konsulenthus, som bygger avancerede cloudløsninger på Azure med afsæt i markedets hurtigste og mest agile metoder. cVation blev stiftet i 2014, har modtaget tre Børsen Gazeller og vundet Microsofts Partner Award for App Innovation tre år i træk. Vi arbejder dedikeret med agile arbejdsmetoder, DevOps og Test Driven Development. Vi sætter en ære i at levere software af den højeste kvalitet, og tager desuden principperne fra DevOps og software engineering med i vores dataprojekter, hvilket betyder, at tests, Infrastructure as Code og automatisering er i fokus.

Du kommer til at arbejde på et af vores dataprojekter med et projektteam bestående af arkitekter, softwareudviklere og DevOps‑specialister. Du vil have fuldt fokus på ét projekt og være tilknyttet ét team ad gangen.
Din rolle bliver at:
rådgive om og bygge datawarehouse- og dataplatformsløsninger i skyen
bidrage til design og implementering af den optimale Azure‑infrastruktur
sikre, at alt opsætning og provisionering sker som Infrastructure as Code, fx Terraform
arbejde med fuldautomatiseret overvågning, drift og skalering af løsninger
Hvad forventer vi af dig?
Du har en videregående uddannelse i software engineering, human centered AI el. lign. fra DTU, ITU eller KU samt erfaring i data engineering
Du har erfaring med at bygge datawarehouses og dataplatforme og har lyst til at arbejde med moderne data engineering

Du har erfaring med – og vil gerne blive klogere på – flere af følgende teknologier: Fabric, Databricks, Azure Data Factory, Spark, Data Warehouse, Data Platform, Python, Azure DevOps, PowerShell, Azure CLI, YAML, Bicep, Terraform, Git og CI/CD
Du vil gerne arbejde med de nyeste teknologier i Azure
Du vil gerne indgå i et agilt setup, hvor krav og retning hurtigt kan ændres, men hvor medbestemmelse og frihed er i højsædet
Det vil være en fordel, hvis du er vant til at arbejde som konsulent i et udviklingsteam i tæt samarbejde med arkitekter og software udviklere
Du har evnen til at omsætte kundebehov til handling
Du er flydende i skrift og tale på dansk og engelsk, og trives med at have den daglige kontakt med kunden

Som person er du selvstændig, ansvarsbevidst, initiativrig, eksekverende og serviceminded. Du er drevet af at lære nyt og vild med at holde dig ajour med de nyeste metoder og teknologier. Du er engageret og motiveret i alt hvad du foretager dig. Hvis du også er glad for at sparre med dygtige kollegaer og trives med at løfte i flok, vil du passe perfekt ind hos os.
Hvad tilbyder vi?

Du kommer til at indgå i vores voksende team, hvor høj faglighed, innovation og arbejdsglæde er i højsæde. Det høje faglige niveau blandt dine kollegaer giver en unik mulighed for at arbejde med de bedste, og vi tager din faglige udvikling alvorligt. Vi har en flad organisation med et minimum af administration, hvor ledelsens døre altid er åbne, og der er god plads til både bordfodbold, fredagsbar, frokost på kajen og et aktivt liv udenfor arbejdstid. Flere gange om året afholder vi hackathons, hvor vi i teams dykker ned i præcis de emner, som udviklerne finder interessante. En del af vores kultur er også, at vi gerne ser alle komme med input til hvordan vi kan optimere, udvikle og forbedre cVation.
Hvorfor vælge os som din nye arbejdsplads?

Vores fundament er løsninger bygget på Microsoft Azure Cloud. Alle vores medarbejdere mestrer cloud-native udvikling og DevOps. Vi arbejder i projekter og engagerer os hands-on i fælles teams med vores kunder for at sikre kvalitet og effektiv videndeling.

Vi er passionerede omkring at hjælpe vores kunder med at få det maksimale ud af cloudteknologien. For os er cloud ikke blot infrastruktur eller et nyt datacenter i skyen. Teknologien giver virksomheder mulighed for at skabe væsentligt mere værdi og arbejde med agilitet og innovation på en helt ny måde.

Vi har været med fra starten, da cloud blev udviklet og tilgængeligt. Vi tilbyder således vores årelange erfaring og dybe cloud ekspertise samt en værktøjskasse med alle vores best practices i form af accelerationsplatformen CADD. Den dækker over gennemprøvede værktøjer, rammeværk, effektive udviklingsmetoder og færdige automatiseringskomponenter. Alt dette for at sikre, at projekter kommer hurtigt og sikkert fra start.

Du vil blive en del af en virksomhed, som tror på, at alle har betydning, og at det skal være sjovt at gå på arbejde hver dag. Du vil få kollegaer, der er interesseret i at lære og har ønske om at blive de bedste til det, de gør. Vi tilbyder derfor gode muligheder for at udvikle dig.
Interesseret?

Klik på Easy Apply knappen, hvis du vil arbejde med de nyeste cloud-teknologier. Hvis du har under 5 års erfaring, bedes du også vedhæfte dine karakterblade i samme fil som dit CV. Vi behandler din ansøgning fortroligt og arrangerer løbende interviews.
Har du spørgsmål til jobbet, eller vil du høre mere om cVation, er du meget velkommen til at kontakte:
Maiken Pelby, Head of People & Culture, T/ 42612828, map@cvation.com
Vi ser frem til at høre fra dig!

<section data-max-lines=""5"" class=""show-more-less-html"">
<div class=""show-more-less-html__markup show-more-less-html__markup--clamp-after-5
relative overflow-hidden"">

Azure Data Engineer


Som Data Engineer hos cVation bygger du innovative dataplatforme og datawarehouses i Azure. Du arbejder med ETL-processer og automatisering af dataflows, og du bygger skalerbare, driftssikre løsninger for vores kunder. Vi bruger teknologier som Fabric, Databricks, Spark, Azure Data Factory, Terraform, Git og CI/CD. Fokus er på data engineering, pipelines og cloud-infrastruktur.


cVation er et dansk udviklings- og konsulenthus, som bygger avancerede cloudløsninger på Azure med afsæt i markedets hurtigste og mest agile metoder. cVation blev stiftet i 2014, har modtaget tre Børsen Gazeller og vundet Microsofts Partner Award for App Innovation tre år i træk. Vi arbejder dedikeret med agile arbejdsmetoder, DevOps og Test Driven Development. Vi sætter en ære i at levere software af den højeste kvalitet, og tager desuden principperne fra DevOps og software engineering med i vores dataprojekter, hvilket betyder, at tests, Infrastructure as Code og automatisering er i fokus.


Du kommer til at arbejde på et af vores dataprojekter med et projektteam bestående af arkitekter, softwareudviklere og DevOps‑specialister. Du vil have fuldt fokus på ét projekt og være tilknyttet ét team ad gangen.


Din rolle bliver at:

  • rådgive om og bygge datawarehouse- og dataplatformsløsninger i skyen
  • bidrage til design og implementering af den optimale Azure‑infrastruktur
  • sikre, at alt opsætning og provisionering sker som Infrastructure as Code, fx Terraform
  • arbejde med fuldautomatiseret overvågning, drift og skalering af løsninger


Hvad forventer vi af dig?

  • Du har en videregående uddannelse i software engineering, human centered AI el. lign. fra DTU, ITU eller KU samt erfaring i data engineering
  • Du har erfaring med at bygge datawarehouses og dataplatforme og har lyst til at arbejde med moderne data engineering
  • Du har erfaring med – og vil gerne blive klogere på – flere af følgende teknologier: Fabric, Databricks, Azure Data Factory, Spark, Data Warehouse, Data Platform, Python, Azure DevOps, PowerShell, Azure CLI, YAML, Bicep, Terraform, Git og CI/CD
  • Du vil gerne arbejde med de nyeste teknologier i Azure
  • Du vil gerne indgå i et agilt setup, hvor krav og retning hurtigt kan ændres, men hvor medbestemmelse og frihed er i højsædet
  • Det vil være en fordel, hvis du er vant til at arbejde som konsulent i et udviklingsteam i tæt samarbejde med arkitekter og software udviklere
  • Du har evnen til at omsætte kundebehov til handling
  • Du er flydende i skrift og tale på dansk og engelsk, og trives med at have den daglige kontakt med kunden


Som person er du selvstændig, ansvarsbevidst, initiativrig, eksekverende og serviceminded. Du er drevet af at lære nyt og vild med at holde dig ajour med de nyeste metoder og teknologier. Du er engageret og motiveret i alt hvad du foretager dig. Hvis du også er glad for at sparre med dygtige kollegaer og trives med at løfte i flok, vil du passe perfekt ind hos os.


Hvad tilbyder vi?

Du kommer til at indgå i vores voksende team, hvor høj faglighed, innovation og arbejdsglæde er i højsæde. Det høje faglige niveau blandt dine kollegaer giver en unik mulighed for at arbejde med de bedste, og vi tager din faglige udvikling alvorligt. Vi har en flad organisation med et minimum af administration, hvor ledelsens døre altid er åbne, og der er god plads til både bordfodbold, fredagsbar, frokost på kajen og et aktivt liv udenfor arbejdstid. Flere gange om året afholder vi hackathons, hvor vi i teams dykker ned i præcis de emner, som udviklerne finder interessante. En del af vores kultur er også, at vi gerne ser alle komme med input til hvordan vi kan optimere, udvikle og forbedre cVation.

4432763793,https://dk.linkedin.com/jobs/view/data-scientist-at-koda-staff-4432763793?position=29&pageNum=0&refId=CfMIyVopItBTcE2aNO1GiA%3D%3D&trackingId=rhLAOIQdRQHeQAxCkVIh6g%3D%3D,Data Scientist,Koda Staff,"Aarhus, Central Denmark Region, Denmark","I am working with a leading global quantitative investment firm that is looking to hire a Data Scientist to join its Commodities business in Aarhus.

This is a front-office facing role sitting at the intersection of Research, Trading, and Engineering, focused on transforming complex datasets into high-value research and trading inputs used across systematic commodities strategies.

The successful candidate will work closely with traders, quantitative researchers, and data engineers to onboard, transform, and productionise both traditional and alternative datasets that directly support investment decisions.
What you'll be working on
Partnering with Trading and Research teams to understand and deliver data requirements
Designing and implementing scalable data pipelines supporting systematic commodities strategies
Onboarding and integrating new datasets into the firm's research platform
Developing data transformation processes to create high-value, investment-ready datasets
Building tools and workflows to extract, clean, normalise, and aggregate data from diverse raw sources and formats
Managing the end-to-end lifecycle of new data acquisitions and integrations
Driving data quality, validation, and monitoring across critical research datasets
Troubleshooting complex data challenges and reducing time-to-production for new data sources
Researching and implementing innovative methods for data extraction and acquisition
Working closely with stakeholders across Trading, Research, Engineering, and external data vendors
This is a highly visible position offering direct exposure to front-office teams and the opportunity to work on some of the most interesting data challenges within quantitative investing.
What they're looking for
3+ years of experience in Data Science, Data Engineering, Quantitative Analytics, or a related data-focused role
Advanced Python programming skills, including strong experience with Pandas and NumPy
Experience designing and maintaining data pipelines and data transformation workflows
Experience working with financial, market, commodities, or alternative datasets
Postgraduate degree in Mathematics, Physics, Engineering, Computer Science, Statistics, or another quantitative discipline
Demonstrable interest in financial markets and data-driven investment processes
Strong problem-solving skills and the ability to operate in a fast-paced, high-performance environment
Excellent communication skills with the ability to collaborate effectively across technical and business teams
Prior experience within systematic trading, quantitative finance, hedge funds, asset management, commodity trading, market data, or alternative data environments would be advantageous
Please apply directly.
They are open to people looking to relocate, but you must have the expertise described above.

4430495152,https://dk.linkedin.com/jobs/view/student-data-engineer-at-implement-consulting-group-4430495152?position=30&pageNum=0&refId=CfMIyVopItBTcE2aNO1GiA%3D%3D&trackingId=TxhW6HrnnerZTMbkXzeBxw%3D%3D,Student Data Engineer,Implement Consulting Group,"Gentofte, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark","Do you enjoy working with data – building reliable foundations and turning complex information into clear insights? At Implement Consulting Group, we're looking for a Student Data Engineer to join our internal team and help develop our data platforms and analytics capabilities further.
What you’ll do
It's a hands-on opportunity for a curious, motivated student who wants to work with data, systems, and business challenges – and to grow into data engineering alongside experienced colleagues.
You’ll support the development and maintenance of our internal data foundation – helping ensure that data across Implement is structured, reliable, and accessible for analysis and decision-making.
Your work will include, but not be limited to:
Transforming and preparing data for analytics and reporting using SQL and Python
Helping develop, maintain, and optimise our data pipelines
Contributing to data modelling and structuring of datasets
Working with tools such as Power BI and modern data platforms
Collaborating with colleagues across teams to understand data needs and ensure quality
The role sits at the intersection of data engineering and analytics
, working with data from multiple source systems. You’ll work closely with experienced data engineers and consultants in a team where learning, sparring, and quality come first.
What you’ll bring
We’re looking for someone who is structured, curious, and motivated to learn. You take ownership of your tasks, ask questions when needed, and enjoy working with data.
You bring:
A solid foundation in SQL, including querying, transforming, and validating data
Experience with Power BI or similar analytics tools
A basic understanding of ETL/ELT processes
Familiarity with data modelling concepts, such as star schemas, fact tables, and dimension tables
A willingness to learn new tools and technologies quickly
Basic experience with Python for data ingestion and/or processing
It is also an advantage if you have:
Experience with cloud data platforms such as Azure, Microsoft Fabric, Databricks, or Snowflake
Knowledge of data orchestration tools such as Azure Data Factory, Fabric Pipelines, Airflow, or dbt
Experience with Git or version control tools
A basic understanding of APIs, JSON, and data integration
What we offer

At Implement, you’ll be part of a collaborative and ambitious environment where we take both learning and quality seriously. We believe in combining freedom with ownership, collaboration without hierarchy, and a strong focus on learning and long-term impact.
You can expect:
Hands-on experience with data engineering in practice
Work with modern data platforms and tools
Support from experienced colleagues who are invested in your development
Responsibility early on — with the right support and sparring
The opportunity to grow your role over time
How to apply
Please submit your application ASAP, as we review applications and conduct interviews on an ongoing basis.
Apply now and take the next step in your data journey with Implement.
Make your next move matter
