To help our clients succeed with their most critical change initiatives, we aim to attract, develop, and promote the best talent by creating a unique, collaborative learning environment where people can follow their passion, shape their own careers, and choose their own leaders. and choose their own leaders.

4434476793,https://dk.linkedin.com/jobs/view/audience-insights-specialist-at-dfds-4434476793?position=34&pageNum=0&refId=CfMIyVopItBTcE2aNO1GiA%3D%3D&trackingId=33FdxWFTl5YfjYsV13EAFA%3D%3D,Audience Insights Specialist,DFDS,"Copenhagen Municipality, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark","Do you want to be a valued part of the critical infrastructure that serves communities across Europe and beyond? Are you looking for a position in which you can set direction and help shape our company? If so, then you could be the newest addition to our team as Audience Insight Specialist.
Be part of the movement – and play your part
About The Role

We serve b2c Ferry passengers globally and we are seeking an analytical thinker with strong strategic vision. You should be capable of stakeholder management, with a strong background in turning data into insight and delivering innovative value-added analysis.

It is imperative you are someone that is proactive and passionate about insights. You will need to have a technical skill set, a thorough understanding of manipulating raw data and building this into actionable insight using analytical and visualisation tools.
You Will Be Responsible For
Support the Insights strategy through the building of reports that answer key business questions and track the performance of success metrics
Support the passenger audience segmentation strategy, definitions and tactics for activation, through incorporating audience segmentation splits within reporting
Support the Audience Insights Lead in mapping the passenger customer journeys and build reports to measure efficiency at specific touchpoints in the customer journey
Ensure insights are available across all markets and routes and aligned with business needs
Support the Audience Insights and Strategic Development team in identifying business opportunities through the identification of trends within reporting
Guide your colleagues in any research needs, aligned to the trends/ opportunities identified through Audience Insights reporting
Adopt cross-functional alignment: partner with revenue, marketing, sales and routes to ensure reporting and metrics are standardised
You will be data literate and have a strong ability to identify trends alongside an inquisitive nature to understand the root cause of specific trends identified.
Your Capabilities
Strong analytical capabilities with experience in visualisation tools such as Power BI and preferably also data connectivity tools such as SQL or similar
Logical thinker with the ability to understand business needs
Business acumen: tie insights to revenue, retention, product-market fit, and prioritization/ROI
Storytelling and influencing without authority, utilising data to provide actionable insights
Presentation and communication skills – complex is easy, simple is hard
Experience with surveys and survey design
Experience with brand tracking and/or online panel/communities is a plus
About You

You will work in the Audience Insights and Strategic Development team, utilising the data available to you to build a reporting suite that is aligned to business needs, generating the insights needed to guide strategic decisions. You will work alongside colleagues aligning reporting with research projects and programmes (e.g. The Voice of The Passenger – NPS and CSAT) to provide holistic actionable insights. You will be a very analytical individual that is excited by turning data into insights and capable to utilise tools to improve data connectivity and increase automation across reports. You will be an enthusiastic, curious, passionate and collaborative individual who continually looks for opportunities, with a solution focussed attitude.
Skills And Experience We Imagine You Bring
An analytical thinker with the ability to turn data into insights
2-3 years of insight and data & analytics experience (essential)
Preferably technical with experience in Power BI or similar tool and maybe also SQL, python, and/or other relevant tools
Strong organisational skills with the ability to prioritise own and others workload.
Ability to communicate complex data and ideas into digestible information.
Ability to be flexible, and adapt to change and leverage trends
Strong communication and collaboration skills
We think the strongest teams are built on diversity and inclusivity. Contrasting opinions, varied skills and strong characters bring out the best in us all. And we cannot wait for you to join.
Working at DFDS
Be a valued member of a diverse team at DFDS and help drive change together.
We promote a great workplace with a diverse and vibrant community and a healthy work–life balance. The position can be located in either Newcastle or Copenhagen.

While you’re with us, we want you to develop. At DFDS, we believe that development happens first and foremost through activities in the workplace. We will also offer training courses that can support you in your role, if needed.

Do you want to be a valued part of the critical infrastructure that serves communities across Europe and beyond? Are you looking for a position in which you can set direction and help shape our company? If so, then you could be the newest addition to our team as Audience Insight Specialist.

Be part of the movement – and play your part

About The Role

We serve b2c Ferry passengers globally and we are seeking an analytical thinker with strong strategic vision. You should be capable of stakeholder management, with a strong background in turning data into insight and delivering innovative value-added analysis.

It is imperative you are someone that is proactive and passionate about insights. You will need to have a technical skill set, a thorough understanding of manipulating raw data and building this into actionable insight using analytical and visualisation tools.

You Will Be Responsible For

  • Support the Insights strategy through the building of reports that answer key business questions and track the performance of success metrics
  • Support the passenger audience segmentation strategy, definitions and tactics for activation, through incorporating audience segmentation splits within reporting
  • Support the Audience Insights Lead in mapping the passenger customer journeys and build reports to measure efficiency at specific touchpoints in the customer journey
  • Ensure insights are available across all markets and routes and aligned with business needs
  • Support the Audience Insights and Strategic Development team in identifying business opportunities through the identification of trends within reporting
  • Guide your colleagues in any research needs, aligned to the trends/ opportunities identified through Audience Insights reporting
  • Adopt cross-functional alignment: partner with revenue, marketing, sales and routes to ensure reporting and metrics are standardised
  • You will be data literate and have a strong ability to identify trends alongside an inquisitive nature to understand the root cause of specific trends identified.

Your Capabilities

  • Strong analytical capabilities with experience in visualisation tools such as Power BI and preferably also data connectivity tools such as SQL or similar
  • Logical thinker with the ability to understand business needs
  • Business acumen: tie insights to revenue, retention, product-market fit, and prioritization/ROI
  • Storytelling and influencing without authority, utilising data to provide actionable insights
  • Presentation and communication skills – complex is easy, simple is hard
  • Experience with surveys and survey design
  • Experience with brand tracking and/or online panel/communities is a plus

About You

You will work in the Audience Insights and Strategic Development team, utilising the data available to you to build a reporting suite that is aligned to business needs, generating the insights needed to guide strategic decisions. You will work alongside colleagues aligning reporting with research projects and programmes (e.g. The Voice of The Passenger – NPS and CSAT) to provide holistic actionable insights. You will be a very analytical individual that is excited by turning data into insights and capable to utilise tools to improve data connectivity and increase automation across reports. You will be an enthusiastic, curious, passionate and collaborative individual who continually looks for opportunities, with a solution focussed attitude.

Skills And Experience We Imagine You Bring

  • An analytical thinker with the ability to turn data into insights
  • 2-3 years of insight and data &

Partnering with Trading and Research teams to understand and deliver data requirements

Onboarding and integrating new datasets into the firm's research platform
Developing data transformation processes to create high-value, investment-ready datasets
Building tools and workflows to extract, clean, normalise, and aggregate data from diverse raw sources and formats
Managing the end-to-end lifecycle of new data acquisitions and integrations
Driving data quality, validation, and monitoring across critical research datasets
Troubleshooting complex data challenges and reducing time-to-production for new data sources
Researching and implementing innovative methods for data extraction and acquisition

Postgraduate degree in Mathematics, Physics, Engineering, Computer Science, Statistics, or another quantitative discipline

Strong problem-solving skills and the ability to operate in a fast-paced, high-performance environment

Highly competitive base salary
Discretionary performance bonus
Comprehensive benefits package
Relocation support available where appropriate
The search is active, and the firm is looking to move quickly for high-calibre candidates.

All applications will be treated in the strictest confidence.

4433233745,https://dk.linkedin.com/jobs/view/senior-data-portfolio-analyst-for-markets-data-office-at-saxo-bank-4433233745?position=36&pageNum=0&refId=CfMIyVopItBTcE2aNO1GiA%3D%3D&trackingId=u4hUvgvHBJh8cRIU9ekNzQ%3D%3D,Senior Data Portfolio Analyst for Markets Data Office,Saxo Bank,"Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark","Looking for your next career opportunity within Data Governance, where you can expand your critical data expertise in a fintech organisation?
Drive data quality and governance in Markets

At Saxo, we are building the Markets Data Office as part of the Data Governance Programme. The function is structured across four domain areas Products, Operations, Counterparty Risk, and Market Risk to strengthen how critical data is controlled and used across trading, operations, and risk.

As a Senior Data Portfolio Analyst, you will take a leading role within one of these domain areas. You will help translate governance standards into practical execution, while contributing to the overall way of working across the Markets Data Office. While your focus will be within a specific domain area, you will operate as part of a single Markets Data Office with shared standards, tooling, and governance practices.
This role is ideal for someone who combines strong data expertise with business understanding particularly within financial services or markets-related domains.
What You Will Do

You will play a central role in the Markets Data Office and take responsibility for data quality and governance within a defined domain area. You will help establish how critical data is described, controlled, monitored, and improved in practice.
Your Responsibilities Will Include
Lead the identification and prioritisation of Critical Data Elements (CDEs) and Critical Data Attributes (CDAs) in your domain
Define data quality requirements, thresholds, and tolerances, and translate them into workable controls and monitoring
Drive investigation and remediation of complex data issues across systems, journeys, and stakeholder groups
Act as a key partner to business and technology stakeholders on prioritisation, control design, and governance adoption
Ensure documentation, control evidence, and governance artefacts are clear enough to support transparency, auditability, and ongoing reporting
Help shape the operating model of the new Markets Data Office by building repeatable ways of working across your portfolio area
The role requires strong ownership of documentation and governance artefacts, ensuring transparency, auditability, and consistency across the data landscape.
Who You Are
You bring both technical data expertise and strong business understanding:
Experience in data governance, data quality, data models or advanced data analysis
Experience within financial services, markets, or risk domains is highly valued
Strong understanding of how data supports business processes and outcomes
Proven ability to work independently and drive initiatives end-to-end
Strong stakeholder management and communication skills
Ability to translate complex data issues into clear insights and actions
Strong documentation discipline and attention to detail
You are comfortable working close to data quality tooling, metadata/catalogue capabilities, and technical teams responsible for implementing controls
Technical skills (SQL, Python
column2: Databricks, Great Expectation or similar platforms are a plus)

We are committed to creating an inclusive workplace where different perspectives are valued. We encourage applications from all backgrounds and experiences.

  • Define data quality requirements, thresholds, and tolerances, and translate them into workable controls and monitoring

Who You Are

4432554387,https://dk.linkedin.com/jobs/view/student-research-assistant-part-time-at-yougov-4432554387?position=39&pageNum=0&refId=CfMIyVopItBTcE2aNO1GiA%3D%3D&trackingId=SH2u2f8AzFcaj1O6rS5O8A%3D%3D,Student Research Assistant (part-time),YouGov,"Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark","YouGov is an international online research data and analytics technology group. Our innovative solutions help the world’s most recognised brands, media owners and agencies to plan, activate and track their marketing activities better. With operations in the UK, the Americas, Europe, the Middle East, India and Asia Pacific, we have one of the world's largest research networks. At the core of our platform is an ever-growing source of connected consumer data that has developed daily over our 20 years of operation. We call it living data. All of our products and services draw upon this detailed understanding of our 20+ million registered panel members to deliver accurate, actionable consumer insights. Job description You will be supporting fast-turnaround research projects on many different topics, which is a part of YouGov’s Client Service offering.

As Student Research Assistant, you will support Research Executives on various tasks. The role relies on excellent communication skills in Norwegian, Danish and English. You’ll be working closely with other YouGov teams as well as directly with clients to design and deliver research which is in line with the brief.
Your primary responsibility will be:
Proof-reading of client-based questionnaires.
Checking and adjustment of questionnaire scripting instructions.
Scripting of surveys in YouGov’s own self-service tool.
Checking of questionnaire test links before launch.
Data output checking and delivery for clients.
Supporting on custom report preparations.
These are the essentials you’ll need to get an interview:
Danish, Norwegian and English in writing/speaking.
Working proficiency in the basic MS package.
Strong attention to details and the ability to deliver results and meet deadlines.
Problem-solving, analytical and numerical skills.

This is a part-time, paid vacancy of maximum 20 hours per week for student candidates. It is hybrid working arrangement of 40% in office, 60% remote working. The salary is DKK150 per hour, with a pay review after 6 months.
Why join YouGov?
Join our global team to help us achieve our social mission: to make millions of people’s opinions heard for the benefit of our local, national, and international communities.

Understanding diversity of opinion requires diversity of background. Although our global panel of millions of people worldwide powers our research, our biggest asset is our people. If our research is to be truly representative of what the world thinks, we need people from all walks of life to be part of the team to bring their perspective to the work we do.
Life at YouGov

We are driven by a set of shared values. We are fast, fearless, and innovative. We work diligently to get it right. We are guided by accuracy, ethics, and proven methodologies. We respect and trust each other, bringing these values into everything that we do.

We strive to provide YouGovers with best-in-class benefits to support their physical, financial, and emotional wellbeing. We want our employees to have a sense of belonging and uniqueness in a supportive workplace, so they can bring their full selves to work.
Equal Opportunity Employer

As an Equal Opportunity Employer, qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex (including pregnancy, gender identity or expression, and sexual orientation), parental status, national origin, marital status, age, disability, genetic information, HIV status, political affiliation, socioeconomic background, veteran status or any other characteristic protected by law or in line with our responsibilities as a fair and ethic employer. All employment decisions are made based on occupational qualifications, merit, and business need.
Data Privacy
To find out how we collect and use your personal data when you apply for a role at YouGov, please read our privacy notice at https://jobs.yougov.com/privacy

Your Responsibilities
Pipeline & Ingestion
Design, build, and maintain scalable ELT pipelines that bring data from product backends, third-party services, and operational systems into Amazon Redshift.

Data Platform & Infrastructure
Evolve Spirii's data platform architecture: storage, compute, access control, and cost optimisation in Redshift.
Implement and maintain infrastructure-as-code practices for data workflows (Terraform/CDK or similar).
Build and maintain CI/CD pipelines for data infrastructure and transformations.
Data Quality & Governance

Ensure GDPR-compliant data handling: PII identification, access control, and data retention policies.

Collaborate with Data team to ensure the analytics layer is built on reliable, well-modelled foundations.

You care deeply about data reliability and building pipelines that teams can depend on.
You're hands-on: you build, test, and ship pipelines and infrastructure improvements yourself.
You think of data pipelines as products — you care about uptime, observability, and the downstream consumers who depend on them.
You're pragmatic: you know when to engineer robustly and when to keep things lean.
You collaborate well across teams and communicate clearly about trade-offs and status.

Bachelor's or Master's degree in Computer Science, Data Engineering, or a related field (or equivalent experience).
3+ years of experience in data engineering or a related role with a strong data focus.
Strong SQL skills and solid experience building production data pipelines.
Hands-on experience with a modern data warehouse (Redshift preferred
column2: BigQuery/Snowflake/Databricks also relevant).

Experience with finance data and/or subscription/transaction domains.
Experience with dbt (understanding of transformation layer and how it interfaces with pipelines).
Machine learning infrastructure experience is a plus, but not required for this role.
Benefits

It is important for us to create a healthy, diverse, fun, and creative working environment, so besides all the basic benefits, we also focus a lot on social activities, flexible working hours and a good work-life balance.
Flexible Work Environment: Enjoy a collaborative, inclusive, and open workplace designed to fit your needs and support work-life balance.

Two innovation days per month for YOU to choose what you work on.

4432364126,https://dk.linkedin.com/jobs/view/data-engineer-at-omnidocs-4432364126?position=44&pageNum=0&refId=CfMIyVopItBTcE2aNO1GiA%3D%3D&trackingId=mjGmxQYbXiv6SKdSxJA3Gg%3D%3D,Data Engineer,Omnidocs,"Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark","We’re looking for a hands-on Data Engineer to join our Copenhagen office and bring structure to a fragmented data landscape - building a foundation that can be used across the business, by people and by AI. As part of our Revenue Operations team, you will work closely with IT and colleagues across the company to make business data consistent, understandable, and trustworthy. You’ll be joining a newly established function in a growing SaaS company.
The role

This is a hands-on, foundational role: practical execution, not data science. That said, no central data platform exists here yet, so “hands-on” includes real decisions about architecture and tooling - this isn’t a job where you just plug into something that’s already built. Dashboards and reporting matter, but they’re an output. The real goal is data structured and reliable enough to power AI, automation, and better decisions.
This position is part of our Revenue Operations team and reports to Martin Rafn Møller. We’re looking for a candidate with around 3+ years of hands-on experience.
What you’ll do
Platform setup
Own the end-to-end design of our data platform
column2: picking and setting up the stack across ingestion, storage, modeling, and delivery.

column1: Data ingestion

Map the business systems where company data lives (CRM, marketing, project delivery, product, support, and other operational platforms), and bring that data in via managed ELT connectors (e.g. Fivetran, Airbyte, or Weld) or custom-built pipelines, depending on what each source supports.

Work with IT on the custom integrations this requires - API ingestion, database connections, or source-specific flows. As a result of ongoing mergers and acquisitions, expect this to be a regular part of the job, not a rare exception.
Modeling, quality, and documentation

Turn raw source data into a clear, maintainable foundation of cleaned, modeled, reusable datasets, with data models that reflect how the business actually works and clear definitions for entities, relationships, and metrics.
Implement practical data quality checks so missing, duplicated, outdated, or inconsistent data is caught before it affects reporting or downstream automation.
Document sources, transformations, metric definitions, ownership, and access so the foundation is trusted across the business.
Reporting, automation, and AI use cases

Prepare data so it’s genuinely AI-ready, meaning structured, clearly defined, documented, and reliable enough that AI can be applied broadly. Not only for analysis, but for automation, assistants, and AI-driven workflows across the business, as well as for human use.
Replace manual exports and conflicting definitions with automated, repeatable data flows - and build dashboards and reports on top of trusted models where they’re needed.
The company

For over 20 years, Omnidocs has built software for document generation. Born as a consultancy and turned into a SaaS business, the company now helps customers worldwide get the best results from their documents and presentations.

Today, the Omnidocs Group has a strong portfolio comprised of several products and almost 200 professionals across various office locations such as Denmark, The Netherlands, UK, Switzerland and more. Following a strategy of acquiring new companies and technologies, in the past 3 years we welcomed Dania Software, Office Consult, Xential, Eformity, Presentation Solutions and officeatwork to the group.
As we mature into a more product- and data-oriented company, getting our data foundation right has become essential to how we operate and how we make decisions. That’s where you come in.
Your qualifications
The essential:
Hands-on experience with data from real business systems, not just sample datasets or isolated reports
Strong SQL for querying, transforming, modeling, and validating data
Python or another scripting language for data processing, automation, and operational data tasks

Experience building data pipelines into a central data platform - using managed ELT connectors (such as Fivetran, Airbyte, or Weld) and custom-built integrations depending on the source, with a solid grasp of ETL/ELT concepts and data quality principles
Experience with a modern data warehouse, lakehouse, or cloud data platform
A clear grasp of how to structure raw data into clean, reusable models that support reporting, analysis, automation, and AI - including a basic sense of what that asks of data quality and structure

Ability to translate unclear business questions into practical data structures and outputs, with good judgment about when to use a standard connector, coordinate something custom, or leave a process manual for now
Strong communication skills in English
It would be great if you also have an interest and/or experience in:

The modern data stack: dbt for modeling and documentation, Fivetran/Airbyte/Weld for ingestion, Snowflake/BigQuery/Fabric/Databricks for the platform, Dagster/Airflow for orchestration, Git for version control
Hands-on experience building or deploying AI-powered features themselves (e.g. a RAG pipeline, an AI agent, or semantic search) - beyond just preparing data for them
Building data foundations where data structures, ownership, and reporting logic are still immature
Building dashboards in tools such as Power BI, Tableau, Looker, or Metabase
Access control, privacy, and basic data governance

Although you must be versatile across the data engineering process, we’re not looking for a “ninja” or “unicorn”: we expect you to know your strengths and be interested in working on your weaker points. Be open about your core competencies, and where you’d like to improve. Our needs are diverse, and we can accommodate different professional profiles.
Soft skills

You are structured, curious, and pragmatic - comfortable with ambiguity and with imperfect systems and incomplete data, creating order over time. You work independently, ask sharp questions, and challenge requirements when the underlying data logic is unclear. You care whether data is useful, reliable, and reusable, not just whether a dashboard looks good. You use modern tools, including AI, naturally in your work - not as a gimmick, but to move faster, improve documentation, debug, and explore data. We appreciate proactivity, adaptability, and a strongly cross-functional, collaborative approach.
What we offer
Flexible work hours: 9-to-5 or 11-to-19, it’s your choice
Hybrid work arrangements
Work-life balance, in true Scandinavian style
Optional lunch scheme in partnership with a local French brasserie
Discounts in gym membership and work glasses
Partially subsidized weekly massage therapy in the office
Social activities like wine tasting, seasonal parties and team events
Sport activities like running groups and participation in the DHL Relay CPH
Career development opportunities
An informal work environment in our office in the heart of the historic Christianshavn neighbourhood, surrounded by the vibrant life of cafes, restaurants, and cultural sights
Why is this a great time to join
You won’t have to advocate for the value of data: from senior management down, there’s clear recognition of why this foundation matters

This is the first step in building out our data capability - you’ll be an important part of it, and you’ll help set the standards for how data engineering fits in as we mature into a product- and data-oriented company
You will directly influence not only the company’s data foundation, but also your own role, tooling, and processes

You’ll be the first dedicated data hire - no data team to lean on day-to-day, but IT is a close, responsive partner for integrations and infrastructure, and you’ll work closely with your manager for sparring and ensuring progress.
We’re not asking you to build this without the right tools - what you need to do the job well is part of the conversation once you’re in the role
Application

Please apply via the dedicated application link with your CV and brief written answers to a few short questions in the application form. That’s all we ask - no cover letter or portfolio needed. Keep your answers short and to the point
column2: if we move forward, we’ll explore more in the interview.

column1: As we strive for a thorough and unbiased recruitment process, we also kindly ask you to:
Use the dedicated application link (do NOT send your resume over e-mail)
Do NOT send a picture of yourself
Do NOT share your age or birth date
Recruitment steps:
First interview - initial screening with Martin Rafn Møller starting from the 27th of July
Second interview - a deeper conversation with the team, plus a take-home behavioural assessment (aprox. 30 min) and a follow-up with People & Culture
Final call to discuss additional matters, if needed
Questions?
Feel free to reach out to Martin at mrm@omnidocs.com.
Applications will be reviewed on an ongoing basis. We’ll do our best to get back to you within 3-4 working weeks, but please be aware that this timeframe may be extended.
Processing of personal data

By registering in our HR system, BambooHR, and uploading both application and CV, you accept that Omnidocs stores the registered data about you and the data included in the uploaded documents. All information is used solely for recruitment purposes and will be deleted within 12 months of upload. Please do not include person-sensitive data in the application and CV, e.g. CPR number, information about race, religion, trade union conditions, and health information. You can read about Omnidocs’ guidelines for processing personal data here:
https://omnidocs.com/processing-of-personal-data-in-the-context-of-recruitment/

  • You’ll be the first dedicated data hire - no data team to lean on day-to-day, but IT is a close, responsive partner for integrations and infrastructure, and you’ll work closely with your manager for sparring and ensuring progress.
  • We’re not asking you to build this without the right tools - what you need to do the job well is part of the conversation once you’re in the role


Application

Please apply via the dedicated application link with your CV and brief written answers to a few short questions in the application form. That’s all we ask - no cover letter or portfolio needed. Keep your answers short and to the point
column3:

column4:

    Applications will be reviewed on an ongoing basis. We’ll do our best to get back to you within 3-4 working weeks, but please be aware that this timeframe may be extended.


    Processing of personal data

    By registering in our HR system, BambooHR, and uploading both application and CV, you accept that Omnidocs stores the registered data about you and the data included in the uploaded documents. All information is used solely for recruitment purposes and will be deleted within 12 months of upload. Please do not include person-sensitive data in the application and CV, e.g. CPR number, information about race, religion, trade union conditions, and health information. You can read about Omnidocs’ guidelines for processing personal data here:

    https://omnidocs.com/processing-of-personal-data-in-the-context-of-recruitment/

Are you a technically curious student with a passion for building things that actually work? Do you thrive when given the freedom to experiment and the responsibility to deliver? Then you might be one of our new AI Explorers.

PanzerGlass is globally recognised as one of the leading brands in screen protection. To support our next wave of digital transformation, we are building a new team, AI Explorers, based at our HQ in Hinnerup and reporting directly to the COO. As part of this team, you'll be hands-on from the start, exploring, building, testing, and deploying practical AI and automation tools that have an immediate impact on how we work.

You will work with different stakeholders across the global organisation and engage in AI projects defined by management. Your tasks will vary, as you will work with specific AI projects or tools on a rotation basis, typically for 2-3 months, depending on need, before taking on a new project.
Key responsibilities
You will take real ownership of AI and automation projects across the business. Your responsibilities will include:
Designing and developing AI-driven tools and automations
Building prototypes and Minimum Viable Products (MVPs) to test new ideas quickly
Integrating AI solutions into existing systems and workflows
Collaborating with business stakeholders to understand requirements and translate them into working solutions
Continuously improving and maintaining implemented solutions
Exploring new technologies, Application Programming Interfaces (APIs), and frameworks relevant to AI
Your profile
You're currently studying towards a bachelor's or master's degree in a technical field. You don't just read about AI, you build with it.
To succeed in this role, you will need:
A genuine interest in AI, automation, and emerging technologies
The ability to work independently and turn ideas into working solutions
A problem-solving mindset and strong attention to detail
It's a plus if you have:
Experience with machine learning or AI frameworks
Familiarity with APIs and system integrations
Knowledge of cloud platforms or automation tools
Previous personal or academic projects involving AI
We offer
A company with best-in-class employee survey results, listed among the best workplaces in Denmark and Europe
An opportunity to work on real AI projects with immediate business impact
The freedom to experiment and build solutions from scratch
Impact on business operation - your work will be seen and felt
A supporting team that values learning and development opportunities
Professional and highly skilled colleagues who set the bar high
Application
We screen and interview candidates on an ongoing basis, and we encourage you to submit your application as soon as possible.
Due to GDPR regulations, we are unable to accept applications sent by email. Please submit your application on our Careers Page.
Start date: As soon as possible.

4435397977,https://dk.linkedin.com/jobs/view/revenue-operations-analyst-at-united-fintech-4435397977?position=49&pageNum=0&refId=CfMIyVopItBTcE2aNO1GiA%3D%3D&trackingId=zck9czrdNRtGYusS0C2DxQ%3D%3D,Revenue Operations Analyst,United Fintech,"Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark","Revenue Operations Analyst

United Fintech is building a global platform that connects financial institutions with modern, scalable technology solutions. Through acquisitions and partnerships, we bring together market-leading fintech products that serve banks, exchange groups, brokerages, and investment firms worldwide.
If you enjoy working with data and have a keen interest in commercial operations within a fast-paced fintech environment, then you might be what we are looking for to join our team in Copenhagen.
What You Will Contribute
We are looking for a Revenue Operations Analyst to join our Revenue Operations team at United Fintech.

United Fintech is scaling fast, and our commercial data needs to scale with it. We are looking for someone who does not just report what happened but helps the business understand why and what to do next.

You will be responsible for defining the analytical roadmap for our commercial departments, ensuring that data is not just reported but used as a competitive advantage to drive growth and efficiency. This means working across our commercial data infrastructure, spanning CRM, BI tooling, and the integrations that connect them, with a clear view of how systems speak to each other and how data flows across the customer journey. You will also be expected to continuously improve how the team operates, using AI tools and automation to make analytical workflows faster and more scalable.

This is a hands-on, highly operational role with real ownership and significant growth potential for someone looking to build expertise in revenue analytics, systems integration, and commercial data architecture within a fast-growing fintech environment.
Key responsibilities include:
Analytics and Insight - Translate commercial data into clear, decision-ready insight for leadership and commercial teams. Cover pipeline, churn, revenue trends, and ad hoc requests as they come.
Analytical Roadmap - Define the analytics agenda for our commercial departments. Identify where data is missing or misread and work proactively to close those gaps.
Systems and Data Integration - Own the data flow between CRM, BI tools, and the rest of the commercial stack. Fix problems at the source, not around them. This is the layer the team has been missing.

Workflow Optimisation – Spot manual and repetitive processes across the commercial data stack and build better alternatives. Use AI tools actively to automate analytical work, improve reporting turnaround, and free up capacity for higher-value tasks.

Data Architecture and Visualisation - Design and maintain the reporting structures that support commercial decision-making. Build scalable dashboards and contribute to our Tableau Next implementation.
Data Quality and Forecasting - Monitor CRM and other commercial data quality, identify issues, and support forecasting with structured analysis and materials for leadership review cycles.

Collaboration - Work with the RevOps team and commercial stakeholders to ensure data and insight serve real decisions. Contribute beyond your primary focus area as part of a small, high-ownership team.
You will bring:
2 to 4 years of experience in RevOps, Sales Ops, analytics, BI, or a technically oriented commercial role. A technical academic background is equally welcome.
Strong commercial understanding. Pipeline coverage, conversion rates, churn, ARR, and NRR are part of how you think.
Hands-on experience with BI tooling such as Tableau, Power BI, or similar.

Proven experience using AI tools such as Claude, ChatGPT or Copilot to optimise workflows and accelerate output, including data analysis, documentation, and process automation. Familiarity with workflow automation tools is a plus.
CRM experience is a requirement. You are comfortable working with CRM objects, fields, and data structures on the reporting and data side. Salesforce specifically is a strong bonus.
A systems thinker who understands how data moves between platforms and how to fix it upstream.
Structured, independent, and direct. You own your work and communicate findings clearly to non-technical audiences.
Location & Working Arrangements
This role is based in Copenhagen. The position is full-time and will be reporting to the Senior Revenue Operations Manager.
You will join a three-person RevOps team and play a key role in building the analytics capability that supports commercial decision-making across United Fintech.
What We offer
Key Role in Growth – Be instrumental in preparing us for the next stage of growth as we expand and integrate new businesses.
Collaborative Environment – Work alongside entrepreneurial minds and experts in the fintech sector, where teamwork and innovation are valued.
Opportunities for Development – Access personal and professional growth opportunities within a fast-paced, dynamic organisation.
Work-Life Balance – Flexibility to balance professional achievements with personal ambitions while contributing to a supportive and inclusive culture.
Engaging Culture – Participate in team-building activities, global events, and opportunities to connect with colleagues.
Lunch at our office in central Copenhagen K
Application Process
Please submit your CV by 20 of July. Applications are reviewed on a rolling basis, so early submissions are encouraged. The first round of interviews is expected to take place end of July.
We encourage candidates of all backgrounds to apply and are committed to fostering a diverse, inclusive, and supportive working environment.
About United Fintech

Founded in 2020 and backed by the industry, United Fintech is a Digital Transformation Platform where global Financial Institutions and cutting-edge technology providers come together to unleash their full potential and enable the future of finance. United Fintech remains on the frontier of innovation by acquiring engineering-led fintechs within Capital Markets, Wholesale Banking, and Wealth Management under a central umbrella. In just over four years, the company has acquired six fintechs. United Fintech has received strategic investments from Barclays, BNP Paribas, Citi, Danske Bank and Standard Chartered and Danish Growth Capital.

4426998042,https://dk.linkedin.com/jobs/view/senior-analyst-in-field-repair-quality-at-lime-4426998042?position=50&pageNum=0&refId=CfMIyVopItBTcE2aNO1GiA%3D%3D&trackingId=yppKDUHi9mQ0F4om1OU8pg%3D%3D,"Senior Analyst, In-Field Repair Quality",Lime,Copenhagen Metropolitan Area,"As a global leader in micromobility, Lime is on a mission to build a future where transportation is shared, affordable and carbon-free. A Time Magazine 100 Most Influential Company, Lime has powered more than one billion rides in close to 30 countries across five continents, spurring a new generation of clean alternatives to car ownership. Learn more at li.me.

Lime is hiring a Senior Analyst to join our Global Operations team. In this role, you'll be at the forefront of improving in-field repair quality performance— developing key metrics, building tools that drive real-time visibility, and delivering targeted analysis that informs operational decisions and strategic opportunities.

You'll serve as the functional bridge between Local Operations and our Product organization, translating operational needs into structured data requirements and tooling improvements. You'll collaborate closely with Local Operations, Product, Data Science, and Hardware teams to help shape the future of Lime's in-field repair quality across all of our operations as we continue to scale.

The ideal candidate is highly analytical, technically strong, and passionate about using data to solve complex problems. You're equally comfortable partnering with local operations teams and product or engineering stakeholders — and you know how to both structure a process and build a dashboard. If you have 3+ years of experience in business analytics or operations, strong data analysis and visualization skills, and a drive to influence outcomes through insight, we'd love to hear from you.

This is a hybrid role open to candidates based anywhere in Europe (London, Berlin, Paris, Munich, or Copenhagen preferred), within 120 minutes of a Lime warehouse. The role requires occasional travel (~10%) to Lime warehouses or other locations for team meetings.
What You’ll Do

Improve visibility into in-field repair quality: Define and maintain metrics to track repair performance, effectiveness, compliance, efficiency, and ROI — and build tooling to surface these insights in real time across operations

Build models and dashboards: Use SQL, Tableau, and spreadsheets to develop repair volume forecasting models, quality compliance dashboards, and performance reports for local operations teams — with robust logic, clearly defined assumptions, and intuitive interfaces

Monitor trends and diagnose performance gaps: Track repair efficiency and quality compliance over time, identify root causes behind variances or emerging trends, and recommend actionable solutions at both tactical and strategic levels

Turn operational complexity into structured clarity: Bridge Local Operations and Product/Tech by converting messy, real-world business needs into crisp specs, user stories, data requirements, and SOPs that teams can actually execute against

Formalize and improve operational processes: Map and document quality workflows, surface inefficiencies, and partner with Local Operations to define and roll out improved standard operating procedures

Collaborate with partner teams to improve efficiency: Provide insight and targeted analysis to troubleshoot issues or highlight opportunities. Partner with Operations leadership and local teams to land recommendations and drive action through to resolution

Drive continuous improvement in data analytics capabilities across global operations: Contribute to a culture of data-driven decision making and analytical excellence through peer collaboration, knowledge sharing, and support
About You
Extremely strong proficiency in data analytics, including advanced SQL, spreadsheet modeling, and data visualization (e.g. Tableau or equivalent).
Bachelor's degree in Business, Mathematics, Engineering, Computer Science, or a related field, or equivalent industry experience
3+ years of experience in business analytics or operations analytics, with demonstrated ability to build reports and dashboards used by operational stakeholders
Experience translating business needs into functional data and reporting requirements, ideally working closely with a Product or Engineering team
Ability to structure and document operational processes, comfortable with ambiguity and working from first principles when standard frameworks don't exist
Curious mindset to spot trends in data, deep-dive root causes, and generate proposals for both tactical and strategic solutions
Clear verbal and written communicator, with strong collaboration and influencing skills across both field teams and technical stakeholders
Preferred Experience
Python, Google Apps Script, or other automation/scripting capabilities are a strong plus
Previous experience in a fast-paced, scaling operations environment (gig economy, last-mile, field services, or similar)
Experience working closely with Product or Engineering teams on tooling/product improvements

If you want to make an impact, Lime is the place for you. Not sure if you meet all the qualifications? If this role excites you we encourage you to apply. Explore all opportunities on our career page.

Lime is proud to be an Equal Opportunity Employer. We believe different perspectives help us grow and achieve more. That’s why we’re dedicated to building and developing a team that reflects a wider range of backgrounds, abilities, identities, and experiences. If you require a reasonable accommodation during the application or hiring process, please email recruiting-operations@li.me for assistance.

  • Formalize and improve operational processes: Map and document quality workflows, surface inefficiencies, and partner with Local Operations to define and roll out improved standard operating procedures
  • Collaborate with partner teams to improve efficiency: Provide insight and targeted analysis to troubleshoot issues or highlight opportunities. Partner with Operations leadership and local teams to land recommendations and drive action through to resolution
  • Drive continuous improvement in data analytics capabilities across global operations: Contribute to a culture of data-driven decision making and analytical excellence through peer collaboration, knowledge sharing, and support

About You

  • Extremely strong proficiency in data analytics, including advanced SQL, spreadsheet modeling, and data visualization (e.g. Tableau or equivalent).
  • Bachelor's degree in Business, Mathematics, Engineering, Computer Science, or a related field, or equivalent industry experience
  • 3+ years of experience in business analytics or operations analytics, with demonstrated ability to build reports and dashboards used by operational stakeholders
  • Experience translating business needs into functional data and reporting requirements, ideally working closely with a Product or Engineering team
  • Ability to structure and document operational processes, comfortable with ambiguity and working from first principles when standard frameworks don't exist
  • Curious mindset to spot trends in data, deep-dive root causes, and generate proposals for both tactical and strategic solutions
  • Clear verbal and written communicator, with strong collaboration and influencing skills across both field teams and technical stakeholders

Preferred Experience

  • Python, Google Apps Script, or other automation/scripting capabilities are a strong plus
  • Previous experience in a fast-paced, scaling operations environment (gig economy, last-mile, field services, or similar)
  • Experience working closely with Product or Engineering teams on tooling/product improvements

If you want to make an impact, Lime is the place for you. Not sure if you meet all the qualifications? If this role excites you we encourage you to apply. Explore all opportunities on our career page.

Lime is proud to be an Equal Opportunity Employer. We believe different perspectives help us grow and achieve more. That’s why we’re dedicated to building and developing a team that reflects a wider range of backgrounds, abilities, identities, and experiences. If you require a reasonable accommodation during the application or hiring process, please email recruiting-operations@li.me for assistance.

4424191816,https://dk.linkedin.com/jobs/view/analytics-engineer-at-vestas-4424191816?position=54&pageNum=0&refId=CfMIyVopItBTcE2aNO1GiA%3D%3D&trackingId=ObFHcoZUc2cskyoe%2F8vfNw%3D%3D,Analytics Engineer,Vestas,"Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark","Join Vestas and help turn data into powerful business insights. As part of our Reporting & Analytics team, you will build scalable data models and dashboards using modern cloud platforms, driving smarter decisions across a global organization.
Finance > Group Financial Performance > Reporting & Analytics CoE

The Reporting & Analytics (R&A) Centre of Excellence is a global function in Finance responsible for enabling data-driven performance management and decision-making across Vestas. The CoE brings together business-facing analytics, governance and definitions, analytics engineering, and operations into a single, integrated operating model. Its purpose is to create a scalable, standardised, and high-quality analytics environment that delivers “one version of the truth” across Vestas. The R&A CoE works closely with Finance, Commercial, Operations, and Digital to translate business needs into analytics products, while ensuring consistency in data definitions, efficient delivery, and modern engineering practices. By combining subject matter knowledge, technical capability, and operational excellence, the CoE plays a critical role in improving performance transparency, accelerating decision-making, and reducing manual reporting across the enterprise.
Responsibilities

In this role, you will contribute to the development of analytical solutions, including presentation layers with data visualizations, semantic layers, and core analytical data models that support business decision-making. As part of the Analytics Engineering pillar, you will play a key role in building and delivering scalable analytics products.
Design and develop analytical solutions, including dashboards, semantic layers, and data models.

Collaborate closely with Product Owners and Business Analysts to translate business requirements into effective analytics solutions. Work cross-functionally with domain experts, governance teams, and other stakeholders to ensure alignment and consistency.
Contribute to the design and implementation of analytics data architecture.
Develop and enhance the core layers of analytical solutions (data models, semantic and presentation layers).
Maintain, optimize, and continuously improve existing data models and analytics solutions.
Support the rollout of analytics solutions, including validation, testing, documentation, and training.
Drive performance optimization and efficient maintenance of analytics products.
Qualifications
Bachelor’s in Computer Science or a related field, with a preference for specialisation in data analytics or data modelling.
3–5+ years of relevant experience in designing and building analytical solutions
Interest in understanding business processes and domain concepts. Demonstrated ability to translate business requirements into effective analytical solutions.
Hands-on experience with cloud-based data platforms (e.g., Snowflake, Databricks, Microsoft Fabric)
Expertise in data modelling and proficiency in SQL
Experience with data visualization tools, particularly Power BI
Exposure to predictive modelling is considered an advantage
Competencies
Ability to approach problems logically, interpret data effectively, and derive meaningful insights
Proactive in identifying challenges and delivering practical, data-driven solutions
Eagerness to learn, adapt, and explore new tools, technologies, and business areas
High level of accuracy and precision in data handling and solution development
Team player with the ability to work effectively across cross-functional teams
Clear and structured communication, with the ability to explain complex concepts to both technical and non-technical stakeholders
Openness to feedback and a drive to continuously enhance skills, processes, and outcomes
What We Offer

You will gain exposure to a global working environment, collaborating with diverse teams across regions, while working on large-scale, cloud-based analytical platforms. The role offers good opportunities for continuous learning and professional development, supported by access to modern tools and technologies within a forward-looking analytics ecosystem.
Additional Information
Primary work location is Copenhagen, Denmark
We do amend or withdraw our jobs and reserve the right to do so at any time, including prior to the advertised closing date. Please be advised to apply on or before 4th July 2026.
Our commitment to a fair hiring.

At Vestas, we evaluate all candidates solely based on their professional experience, education, and relevant skills. To support a fair recruitment process, we kindly ask that you remove any photos, dates of birth or graduation, gender pronouns, marital status, or other personal information not relevant to the role before submitting your CV/resume. Your CV/resume should focus on your professional and educational background, along with the necessary contact details (email and phone number). We train our hiring teams in inclusive evaluation and regularly review process outcomes to ensure fairness.
DEIB Statement

At Vestas, we recognize the value of diversity, equity, and inclusion in driving innovation and success. We strongly encourage individuals from all backgrounds to apply, particularly those who may hesitate due to their identity or feel they do not meet every criterion. As our CEO states, ""Expertise and talent come in many forms, and a diverse workforce enhances our ability to think differently and solve the complex challenges of our industry"". Your unique perspective is what will help us powering the solution for a sustainable, green energy future.
BEWARE – RECRUITMENT FRAUD
It has come to our attention that there are a number of fraudulent emails from people pretending to work for Vestas. Read more via this link, https://www.vestas.com/en/careers/our-recruitment-process
About Vestas
Vestas is the energy industry’s global partner on sustainable energy solutions. We are specialised in designing, manufacturing, installing, and servicing wind turbines, both onshore and offshore.

Across the globe, we have installed more wind power than anyone else. We consider ourselves pioneers within the industry, as we continuously aim to design new solutions and technologies to create a more sustainable future for all of us. With more than 185 GW of wind power installed worldwide and 40+ years of experience in wind energy, we have an unmatched track record demonstrating our expertise within the field.
With 30,000 employees globally, we are a diverse team united by a common goal: to power the solution – today, tomorrow, and far into the future.

Vestas promotes a diverse workforce which embraces all social identities and is free of any discrimination. We commit to create and sustain an environment that acknowledges and harvests different experiences, skills, and perspectives. We also aim to give everyone equal access to opportunity.

To learn more about our company and life at Vestas, we invite you to visit our website at www.vestas.com and follow us on our social media channels. We also encourage you to join our Talent Universe to receive notifications on new and relevant postings.

4436469477,https://dk.linkedin.com/jobs/view/advanced-data-analyst-with-focus-on-semantic-data-at-novo-nordisk-engineering-at-nne-4436469477?position=55&pageNum=0&refId=CfMIyVopItBTcE2aNO1GiA%3D%3D&trackingId=nLxriXFf0y5jP4EzhIp2Ew%3D%3D,Advanced Data Analyst with focus on semantic data at Novo Nordisk Engineering,NNE,"Virum, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark","Advanced Data Analyst with focus on semantic data for Data Engineering department at Novo Nordisk Engineering

If you’re energised by bringing structure to complex data and enabling a shared understanding across disciplines, and you thrive on bridging business needs with technical solutions, we’d love to hear from you!
Who We Are

We are a leading pharma engineering company shaping the future of API pharma engineering globally. Leveraging the expertise of 2,000+ employees across Denmark, India, and the US, we design, build and optimise pharmaceutical manufacturing facilities that are essential to the health and well-being of patients everywhere.

Our success is driven by passion, innovation, and collaboration — with our customers and across our teams and disciplines. If you share our dedication to a noble cause and our passion for API pharma engineering, then NNE is the place for you.
About The Role
We are looking for an Advanced Data Analyst with focus on semantic data to join our Engineering Data & Systems team of 4 passionate colleagues.

As a team, we are working to strengthen the data foundation across engineering projects and support the transition from document-based to data-driven work. We focus on improving the structure and quality of data and enabling better decision-making through dashboards and visualizations.

You will act as the bridge between business and IT – translating engineering needs into semantic concepts and models, while collaborating closely with Knowledge Graph Engineers in IT responsible for technical implementation.
You will own and drive the semantic agenda from a business perspective, ensuring that our semantic models are usable, adopted, and create real value in projects.
Location Denmark - Virum
Experience Minimum 3 Years
Department Data Engineering & Systems
How You Might Spend Your Days
As a Data Analyst with focus on semantic data, you’ll be:
Defining and driving the semantic data structure from a business perspective, ensuring it reflects real operational needs
Facilitating alignment on terminology, concepts, and relationships across disciplines to enable a shared data understanding
Leading the development of ontology models with domain experts and translate business knowledge into structured, implementable data models
Collaborating closely with IT and Knowledge Engineers to ensure correct and scalable technical implementation
Establishing guidelines and best practices while driving consistent adoption and use of semantic models across projects and teams
Who You Are
We care about who you are as a person. In the end, how you work, and your energy is what impacts the results we achieve as a team. As a person, you are:
Possessing a structured and analytical mindset with the ability to structure complex information into clear models
Thriving in a role that requires a proactive approach and taking ownership of tasks from start to finish
A skilled facilitator and communicator that can translate between business needs and technical representation
Comfortable working across disciplines with both internal and external stakeholders
Motivated by creating clarity in changeable and at times complex environment
What You Bring To The Role

In all positions, there are some things that are preferred, and others, a bonus. If you do not meet all the qualifications listed below, you are welcome to offer alternative experience that you think would be valuable in this role:
Minimum 3-5 years of relevant experience within data modelling, semantics, or similar disciplines
Relevant educational degree within data, engineering, IT or similar
Great knowledge of business processes and data structures
Proven experience with stakeholder management across domains including facilitating workshops and driving alignment
Fluency in English
Life at NNE
NNE is short for Novo Nordisk Engineering. And for all of us who work here, it means so much more.

At NNE, you become part of a vibrant, project-based environment built on close day-to-day collaboration. The role is therefore primarily on-site, with physical presence at the workplace expected five days a week. Limited flexibility for occasional remote work may be accommodated in line with business needs.

We are, at our core, a people business and, as such, committed to continuously developing our people and the way we work together. We do not expect you to know it all from day one, and we truly believe a great start makes a real difference. We therefore welcome new colleagues with our award-nominated onboarding programme, supported by a range of in-house talent and development initiatives designed to support your long-term growth.
You can read more about the benefits we offer here .
Did we spark your curiosity? If you’re ready to join our passion, let’s hear from you.
Deadline of application is 22th July 2026
Questions before applying? Send us a quick text to arrange a convenient time to chat. Contact Manager, Mayes Ali on +45 3075 7178 or Director, Niels Karim Høst-Madsen on +45 3075 2705.
Between week 28-30 you can expect a delay in reply due to summer holiday. Therefore, we encourage you to call before Monday 6 July if you have questions.
We kindly refrain from questions regarding the potential fit into the role - we encourage you to apply to the position even if you are a bit in doubt. You might just be the one we are looking for!
If you have any questions regarding the application process, please write to recruit@nne.com and we will assist you as soon as possible.
Please note:
We only accept applications submitted through our online recruitment system
You do not need to attach a cover letter, but please include a few sentences about why you are applying in your resume or CV
We invite to interviews on a running basis.
Fair hiring:

At NNE, we are committed to an inclusive recruitment process and equal opportunities for all job applicants. To maintain fairness, we request that you refrain from including a personal photo in your CV.
Still searching for the right opportunity?

If this position isn’t quite what you're looking for, but you know someone who would excel in this role, please share this job post. And don’t forget to explore our other exciting opportunities — your future role at NNE might be just around the corner! Visit our Career Site

4427022214,https://dk.linkedin.com/jobs/view/business-intelligence-specialist-at-abc-r%C3%A5dgivende-ingeni%C3%B8rer-4427022214?position=60&pageNum=0&refId=CfMIyVopItBTcE2aNO1GiA%3D%3D&trackingId=EYemPQ08KZtzUU%2BKYLc5lQ%3D%3D,Business Intelligence Specialist,ABC - Rådgivende Ingeniører,"Glostrup, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark","AI, automatisering, digitalisering – det er volapyk for de fleste, men for dig er det modersmål. Du motiveres af at omsætte data til vital indsigt og helt konkrete løsninger, og har en stor passion for at fremme anvendelsen af AI.

Hvis det lyder som dig, så er du måske den nye Business Intelligence Specialist, ABC Rådgivende Ingeniører står og mangler! Her vil du ikke blot dagligt varetage, men også videreudvikle vores interne automatiserings- og afrapporteringsplatform, samt bidrage til at modne AI-anvendelse på tværs af virksomheden.
Danmarks (måske) mest digitale ingeniørhus

ABC er vokset meget i de seneste 15 år, vi har eksisteret. I dag tæller vi 200 medarbejdere, varetager alle ingeniørdiscipliner og er undervejs blevet otte Gazellepriser rigere. Vi er gået fra ét specialistområde til at være full-service ingeniørrådgivere, og i de sidste år har vi satset hårdt på digitaliseringen.

Her har ABC solid erfaring med at udvikle og implementere digitale værktøjer igennem vores in-house Digital Innovation-team. Vi har blandt andet været medudviklere på det digitale LCA-værktøj Real-Time LCA, som i 2025 blev solgt til NTI Group, og har udviklet 3D-værktøjet ABConcept, der anvendes til optimering af konstruktioner i de tidlige konceptfaser.

Det er i forlængelse af netop denne ambition, at vi nu søger en selvkørende og struktureret specialist, der både kan arbejde på eget initiativ og i tæt samarbejde med direktion, forretningsledelse og vores forskellige fagmiljøer. Rollen er central for ledelsesrapportering, finans- og HR-automatisering, samt udvikling af datadrevne arbejdsgange på tværs af organisationen.
En autoritet inden for AI og automatisering i ABC

Som Business Intelligence Specialist referer du direkte til partner og forretningschef for Digitalisering, Jesper Wallaert. Derudover vil du arbejde tæt sammen med andre relevante stakeholder i organisationen. Du bliver én af vores go-to specialister og en allround AI- og automatiseringsblæksprutte med sugekopperne viklet rundt om en lang række af virksomhedens vigtigste digitale hjørnesten. Her vil dine arbejdsopgaver bl.a. omfatte:

AI i forretningen: bidrage til udvikling, pilotering og implementering af AI-løsninger i interne processer og projektleverancer fra use case-identifikation til konkret implementering, samt assistere forretningsledelse i at skalere brugen af AI og udvikle nye forretningsområder

Automatisering af finans: videreudvikle og drifte dashboards for direktion, afdelings-, forretnings- og projektledelse, samt sikre stabil dataindlæsning og konsolidering på centrale semantiske modeller.
Ledelsesrapportering: opdatere og udbygge rapporter til forretningsledelses- og direktionsmøder, herunder controller- og budgetrapporter
HR-rapportering: vedligeholde og videreudvikle fraværs- og WorkingHours-rapportering, samt sammenkobling af HR-datakilder
Procesautomatisering: drifte, fejlsøge og udvikle Power Automate-flows (desktop og cloud), herunder interne løsninger som gæsteparkering, ankomstflow mv.
Systemvedligeholdelse: supportere interne platforme (bl.a. Speckle, ABConflict), samt andre mindre infrastruktur- og integrationsopgaver
Du har visionen og får folk til at tro på den

Én ting er, at vi skal have AI- og automatiseringsværktøjerne klar. Noget andet er, at vi skal have folk til at tro på, at de er umagen værd. Ikke nok med, at du kan varetage og videreudvikle vores værktøjer og lede vores udviklingsprojekter, du kan også inspirere og oplære folk til at bruge dem.

Du formår at arbejde egenhændigt når der er behov for det, kan altid skabe dig et godt overblik, selv i de komplekse arbejdsgange, og du er ikke bange for at tage ejerskab over dine løsninger. Men lige så let som du kan køre showet selv, lige så godt trives du i det tætte, tværfaglige samarbejde. Med din innovative tilgang og forretningssans, trives du i krydsfeltet mellem IT, ledelse og forretning.

Her forestiller vi os, at du har min 3-5 års erfaring inden for business intelligence og datadrevet rapportering, samt dokumenteret erfaring og kompetencer inden for AI og automatisering af virksomhedsprocesser og –værktøjer. Relevant brancheerfaring fra rådgivnings-, ingeniør- eller bygge- og anlægsbranchen er en fordel, men er ikke et krav. Udover den rigtige personlighed, ser vi gerne, at du har erfaring med:
Power Bi, herunder DAX, datamodellering og semantiske modeller
Power Platform, særligt Power Automate og Power Apps
Datamodellering & integration, arbejde med API’er, ETL/ELT, datakvalitet og fejlhåndtering
SQL og forståelse for datastrukturerer på tværs af kilder
Microsoft 365 / Azure-stack, herunder SharePoint, Dataverse mm.
Dokumentation og kvalitetssikring
Nok om, hvad du kan gøre for os. Hvad kan ABC tilbyde dig?
Hos ABC går vi op i, at du trives og udvikler dig – både fagligt og personligt. Derfor tilbyder vi blandt andet:
Spændende og udfordrende projekter i en virksomhed med stort fokus på digitalisering og AI
Mulighed for at præge udviklingen af vores BI- og AI-landskab
Tæt samarbejde med direktion og forretningsledelse
Et stærkt fagligt miljø, hvor vidensdeling og sparring er i centrum
En social arbejdsplads med sommer- og julefester, studieture samt sociale klubber inden for fodbold, padel, yoga, cykling, vin og meget mere.
Har vi fanget din opmærksomhed?
Hvis ja, så er vi virkelig spændt på at høre fra dig! Vi indkalder løbende til samtaler, så send os gerne dit CV hurtigst muligt.
Hvis du har uddybende spørgsmål, er du også meget velkommen til at kontakte partner og forretningschef for Digitalisering, Jesper Wallaert, på jwa@abc.dk eller +45 4290 5133.

4426090321,https://dk.linkedin.com/jobs/view/data-science-consultant-copenhagen-at-management-solutions-4426090321?position=1&pageNum=2&refId=9WBE1CWWBPgBIckuV3P5zg%3D%3D&trackingId=%2F8TAsZjmWU1tZZhMMQAhxA%3D%3D,DATA SCIENCE CONSULTANT COPENHAGEN,Management Solutions,Copenhagen Metropolitan Area,"We are currently recruiting for our Summer 2026 intake, with an expected start date in September 2026.

Management Solutions is an international consulting firm whose core mission is to deliver business, risk, financial, organisational and process-related advisory services, targeting both functional aspects and the implementation of related technologies. We currently have a multidisciplinary team (functional, mathematical, technical and systems integration) of 4,000 professionals.

We operate through 52 offices (24 in Europe, 23 in the Americas, 3 in Asia, 1 in Africa and 1 in Oceania) from where we regularly serve clients that operate in more than 50 countries across five major geographical areas (Europe, Americas, Asia, Africa and Oceania).
Role:
Statistical data processing (Data Mining)
Artificial Intelligence (AI), including generative AI
Predictive modeling using Machine Learning and Data Science techniques
Time-series modeling (ARIMA and panel data models) and simulation models (Monte Carlo)
Development, validation and audit of financial, risk, marketing and business-oriented models
R&D projects
Requirements:
Recent graduates or final year students.

Should desirably have knowledge of Artificial Intelligence modeling techniques (logit, GLM, time series, random forests, clustering, XGBoost), statistical programming languages (SAS, R, Python, Matlab), big data tools and platforms (Hadoop, Hive, etc.) and cloud platforms (AWS, Azure, GCP).
Solid academic record.
Strong computer skills.
Postgraduate studies and/or specialized courses are an asset, especially in Data Science, Artificial Intelligence, Quantitative Finance or similar.
Knowledge of other languages is desirable.
Get-up-and-go attitude, maturity, responsibility and strong work ethic.
Strong ability to learn quickly.
Able to integrate easily into multidisciplinary teams.
What we offer:
We offer you the possibility to join a firm that provides all you need to develop your talent to the fullest:
Working in the highest-profile consulting projects in the industry,
for the largest companies, leaders of their respective markets,
alongside top industry management as they face challenges at the national and global level,
as part of an extraordinary team of professionals whose values and corporate culture are a benchmark for the industry
Training:
Ongoing training plan, with approximately 10% of business turnover spent on training.
Specialist knowledge courses, external expert courses, professional skills courses and language courses.
Last year our staff as a whole received over 400,000 hours of training spanning more than 160 courses.
Career plan:
Clearly defined career plan.
Internal promotion based solely on merit.
Partnership-based management model offers all professionals the opportunity to become part of the Firm’s group of partners
Complementary experiences:
University: we maintain a close relationship with the world’s most prestigious universities.
Social Action: we organize more than 30 community support activities.
Sports Club: internal and external tournaments.
How to apply:
If you are interested in our offer, please complete our application form online at https://www.managementsolutions.com/en/professional-careers-send-us-your-cv

4432202559,https://dk.linkedin.com/jobs/view/souschef-fuldtid-holb%C3%A6k-at-lidl-danmark-4432202559?position=5&pageNum=2&refId=9WBE1CWWBPgBIckuV3P5zg%3D%3D&trackingId=xIGc2C8nqMhX1DVJEy8NdQ%3D%3D,Souschef - Fuldtid - Holbæk,Lidl Danmark,"Holbæk, Region Zealand, Denmark","Introduktion
Kan du motivere branchens bedste kolleger og bidrage til, at butikken altid står snorlige og salgsklar? Så er du måske vores nye souschef.

Hos Lidl bliver du en del af Europas største dagligvarekæde med 150+ butikker i Danmark. Sammen er vi drevet af et fælles mål om at blive danskernes førstevalg, og på #teamlidl kommer du med på en succesfuld rejse –– på et hold med høje ambitioner til fælles. Vi har brug for stærke ledere, der giver vores medarbejdere retning og overskud, så vi sammen skaber rammerne for landets bedste indkøbsoplevelse.

Som souschef tager du aktiv del i det daglige arbejde i butikken, og får medansvar for butikkens drift og resultater. Hos os får du ansvar lige fra første arbejdsdag og bliver samtidig en del af et ambitiøst karriereudviklingsforløb. Som Europas største dagligvarekæde har vi nemlig høje ambitioner til fælles – i verden og i hverdagen, med grænseløse muligheder og holdånd i en klasse for sig.

Som souschef i Lidl får du en ansvarsfuld rolle med spændende udfordringer og en konkurrencedygtig lønpakke. Når du starter, klæder vi dig på gennem et grundigt oplæringsforløb i vores koncepter, så du har forudsætningerne for at opnå succes i din nye stilling.
Om jobbet

Som souschef er du med til at drive butikken og lede medarbejderne. Du har medansvar for, at butikken lever op til kundernes forventninger, så de bliver ved med at handle hos os. Ud over at bestille varer, fylde op og ekspedere kunderne ved kassen, får du også følgende opgaver, som du løser i tæt samarbejde med butikschefen:
Planlægning af ressourcer og prioritering af arbejdsopgaver
Oplæring af medarbejdere, så de efterlever Lidls værdier og butikskoncepter
Ledelse af medarbejdere så de lykkes i deres job
Sørge for, at vores butikskoncepter overholdes
Ansvar for butikken når butikschefen ikke er til stede
Om dig

Du er en empatisk og stærk ledertype, der har nemt ved motivere dine medarbejdere. Du bliver respekteret for din faglighed, og du sætter en ære i at tage del i arbejdsopgaverne i den daglige drift, og være med til at sikre, at butikken altid står snorlige og salgsklar.
Du er godt rustet til jobbet, hvis du:
Har brancheerfaring og gerne erfaring fra lignende stilling
Har gennemført et lederkursus eller er motiveret for at udvikle dig som leder
Har en venlig og professionel fremtoning
Inspirerer dine medarbejdere til at følge dit gode eksempel
Har overblik og fokus på detaljen
Ser butikken som et team og ikke er bleg for at give en hånd med, når en kollega har brug for hjælp
Har god forståelse for arbejdsprocesser, procedurer og koncepter
Vi tilbyder
Som souschef i Lidl får du:
En udfordrende rolle, hvor tingene går stærkt
Verdensklasse sammenhold
En konkurrencedygtig lønpakke på 26.000 – 32.500 kr. brutto om måneden
Et udbetalt tillæg i henhold til overenskomsten, når du arbejder på skæve tidspunkter - fx aftener, weekender og helligdage
Løn og vilkår i henhold til gældende overenskomst med HK
En grundig oplæring i vores arbejdsgange og koncepter
Adgang til skræddersyede kurser, der udvikler dig både personligt og fagligt
Mulighed for sundhedsordning
Rabatordning hos flere udbydere af rejser, shopping, kultur, fitness mm.
Vil du med på #teamlidl?
Sådan søger du stillingen:
Klik på ”Søg nu”
Udfyld formularen
Vedhæft dit motiverede CV
Vi rekrutterer løbende, så send dit motiverede CV hurtigst muligt. Da butikken er åben alle ugens dage, vil der forekomme dag-, aften- og weekendarbejde.
Vores rekrutteringsproces består af en telefonsamtale og en fysisk samtale i butikken.

Har du spørgsmål til stillingen eller ansøgningsprocessen, er du velkommen til at kontakte Recruiting på jobilidloest@lidl.dk eller tlf. 7635 0635, mandag – torsdag 8.00 – 15.00/ fredag 08.00 – 14.00.
Vi ser frem til at modtage dit CV!
