4431828083,https://dk.linkedin.com/jobs/view/replenishment-co-worker-for-morning-shifts-30-hours-per-week-gentofte-at-ikea-4431828083?position=6&pageNum=2&refId=9WBE1CWWBPgBIckuV3P5zg%3D%3D&trackingId=04hI87q0EE1k0fI3WYdg5A%3D%3D,"Replenishment co-worker for morning shifts, 30 hours per week, Gentofte",IKEA,"Gentofte Municipality, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark","WHY WE WILL LOVE YOU

Do you love to get up before everybody else and use your physicality in your work? If so, you could be an ideal fit for a Replenishment co-worker position at IKEA. When IKEA opens its doors at 10 AM, it's crucial that the shelves are well-stocked and prepared for our valued customers. This means that logistics here is not only about replenishing goods, but also about providing exceptional customer service and boosting sales.
To excel in this role, you should:
• Thrive in working during the night and early morning hours
• Be in good physical condition and thrive by using your body actively
• Possess a keen eye for detail and enjoy working in an organized and structured manner
• Be able to communicate in English and eager to collaborate effectively with the team.
YOUR RESPONSIBILITIES

Replenishment plays an important part for IKEAs customers and sales, because we make sure our home furnishing accessories are always accessible for our customers. Therefore, we work with a sharp deadline at 10 AM, when the store opens.
Your tasks will be:
• Replenishment all our home furnishing accessories such as plates, pillows, lamps, plants etc. on the sales floor using pallet lifter and manual work
• Working together with colleagues from the whole store to keep the store fully stocked
• Handling item counts as well as updating IT systems accordingly
• Maintenance of machinery and cleaning up the department.
OUR TEAM WITHIN IKEA

Here you will become part of an international team of both fulltime and part-time employees that work towards a common goal. We secure an efficient replenishment of the store and provide good customer service, even though we do not have direct customer contact. We work closely together with all departments, and consequently you gain experience in collaborating with many different types of people. We work in a fast-paced environment, but always support each other and work as a team. Therefore, there is room for you to be exactly who you are, and you will get the opportunity to get a strong social network. We will ensure the correct training, so you will feel safe and competent in the job.
QUESTIONS AND SUPPORT? LET'S CONNECT!

If you have any questions please contact our Logistics Team Leader, Andreas Dalkyriadis via andreas.dalkyriadis@ingka.ikea.com. Please notice that we do not receive applications via e-mail but only through our recruitment portal.
Further information
• This position is for an average of 30 hours per week

• Your working time is primarily in the night and early morning hours, before the store opens. You should also expect to work every other weekend, both Saturday and Sunday. It is possible to give your input to the work schedule
• Salary is within IKEAs banding for logistics and includes pension- and free choice allowance and odd hour payment
• Other employment terms follow HKs Funktionæroverenskomst
• We have various staff benefits including private health insurance, massage, kantine, staff discount, discount club with external partners, yearly gift, bonus etc.
• During the recruitment process, we reserve the right to ask for a criminal record
• Start date is as early as possible
• We have an ongoing interview process and reserve the right to close the job when we have received enough qualified candidates.

Like homes, talent is also diverse, and in IKEA we have a passion for real people. When applying for jobs, some are discouraged if they do not meet every single competence area, but please feel free to still apply. We would love to get to know you as there is a wide range of opportunities in IKEA, based on desire, ability, and experience. We hope you will join us on this adventure and we are looking forward to hearing from you!

4436460852,https://dk.linkedin.com/jobs/view/advanced-data-analyst-to-join-our-data-engineering-department-at-novo-nordisk-engineering-at-nne-4436460852?position=7&pageNum=2&refId=9WBE1CWWBPgBIckuV3P5zg%3D%3D&trackingId=9Sg%2BpWWiD7erNHmPiCUVrg%3D%3D,Advanced Data Analyst to join our Data Engineering department at Novo Nordisk Engineering,NNE,"Virum, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark","If you’re energised by turning complex data into clear insights and you thrive on building structured, scalable BI solutions, we’d love to hear from you!
Who We Are

We are a leading pharma engineering company shaping the future of API pharma engineering globally. Leveraging the expertise of 2,000+ employees across Denmark, India, and the US, we design, build and optimise pharmaceutical manufacturing facilities that are essential to the health and well-being of patients everywhere.

Our success is driven by passion, innovation, and collaboration — with our customers and across our teams and disciplines. If you share our dedication to a noble cause and our passion for API pharma engineering, then NNE is the place for you.
About The Role
We are looking for an Advanced Data Analyst to join our Engineering Data & Systems team of 4 passionate colleagues.

As a team, we are working to strengthen the data foundation across engineering projects and support the transition from document-based to data-driven work. We focus on improving the structure and quality of data and enabling better decision-making through dashboards and visualizations.

You will play a key role in establishing how BI and reporting solutions are developed and managed across our VP area. As we are in an early phase, you will work hands-on across the full lifecycle – from understanding business needs to building and maintaining dashboards and reports. You will act as the go-to person for BI and visualization, supporting teams across our VP area with delivering high-quality, scalable solutions.
Over time, you will help shape standards and ways of working within BI and visualization as the area matures, with the opportunity to take on increased responsibility as the function grows.
Location Denmark - Virum
Experience Minimum 3 Years
Department Data Engineering
How You Might Spend Your Days
As an Advanced Data Analyst, you’ll be:
Leading the development of Power BI dashboards, turning business needs into clear and impactful visualizations
Designing reusable BI templates and ensure consistency in structure, layout, KPIs, and naming
Working with data from the Fabric workspace and apply the right data access and integration approach
Developing and maintain DAX calculations to enable scalable and reliable reporting
Ensuring high quality, performance, and continuous improvement of dashboards while supporting the team in reusing solutions
Who You Are
We care about who you are as a person. In the end, how you work, and your energy is what impacts the results we achieve as a team. As a person, you are:
Thriving by taking ownership, leading tasks from start to finish independently
Great at understanding the ability to collaborate across business and IT
Structured and quality-conscious approach to solving tasks with attention to detail
Pragmatic and solution-oriented mindset, and motived by creating value to the line of business
Able to translate complex data into simple insights and act as a trusted sparring partner to the team
What You Bring To The Role

In all positions, there are some things that are preferred, and others, a bonus. If you do not meet all the qualifications listed below, you are welcome to offer alternative experience that you think would be valuable in this role:
Solid experience (3-5 years) within data analysis or Business Intelligence, combined with a relevant educational background in data, IT, engineering, or similar
Extensive experience in Power BI, including data modelling, DAX, and data visualization
Experience working with modern data platforms (e.g. Fabric, Azure, or similar) and building scalable BI solutions
Great understanding of data flows, data models, and hands-on experience with SQL and data processing
Fluency in English
Life at NNE
NNE is short for Novo Nordisk Engineering. And for all of us who work here, it means so much more.

At NNE, you become part of a vibrant, project-based environment built on close day-to-day collaboration. The role is therefore primarily on-site, with physical presence at the workplace expected five days a week. Limited flexibility for occasional remote work may be accommodated in line with business needs.

We are, at our core, a people business and, as such, committed to continuously developing our people and the way we work together. We do not expect you to know it all from day one, and we truly believe a great start makes a real difference. We therefore welcome new colleagues with our award-nominated onboarding programme, supported by a range of in-house talent and development initiatives designed to support your long-term growth.
You can read more about the benefits we offer here .
Did we spark your curiosity? If you’re ready to join our passion, let’s hear from you.
Deadline of application is 22th July 2026
Questions before applying? Send us a quick text to arrange a convenient time to chat. Contact Manager, Mayes Ali on +45 3075 7178 or Director, Niels Karim Høst-Madsen on +45 3075 2705.
Between week 28-30 you can expect a delay in reply due to summer holiday. Therefore, we encourage you to call before Monday 6 July if you have questions.
We kindly refrain from questions regarding the potential fit into the role - we encourage you to apply to the position even if you are a bit in doubt. You might just be the one we are looking for!
If you have any questions regarding the application process, please write to recruit@nne.com and we will assist you as soon as possible.
Please note:
We only accept applications submitted through our online recruitment system
You do not need to attach a cover letter, but please include a few sentences about why you are applying in your resume or CV
We invite to interviews on a running basis.
Fair hiring:

At NNE, we are committed to an inclusive recruitment process and equal opportunities for all job applicants. To maintain fairness, we request that you refrain from including a personal photo in your CV.
Still searching for the right opportunity?

If this position isn’t quite what you're looking for, but you know someone who would excel in this role, please share this job post. And don’t forget to explore our other exciting opportunities — your future role at NNE might be just around the corner! Visit our Career Site

If you’re energised by turning complex data into clear insights and you thrive on building structured, scalable BI solutions, we’d love to hear from you!

Who We Are

We are a leading pharma engineering company shaping the future of API pharma engineering globally. Leveraging the expertise of 2,000+ employees across Denmark, India, and the US, we design, build and optimise pharmaceutical manufacturing facilities that are essential to the health and well-being of patients everywhere.

Our success is driven by passion, innovation, and collaboration — with our customers and across our teams and disciplines. If you share our dedication to a noble cause and our passion for API pharma engineering, then NNE is the place for you.

About The Role

We are looking for an Advanced Data Analyst to join our Engineering Data &
column2:
Systems team of 4 passionate colleagues.

As a team, we are working to strengthen the data foundation across engineering projects and support the transition from document-based to data-driven work. We focus on improving the structure and quality of data and enabling better decision-making through dashboards and visualizations.

You will play a key role in establishing how BI and reporting solutions are developed and managed across our VP area. As we are in an early phase, you will work hands-on across the full lifecycle – from understanding business needs to building and maintaining dashboards and reports. You will act as the go-to person for BI and visualization, supporting teams across our VP area with delivering high-quality, scalable solutions.

Over time, you will help shape standards and ways of working within BI and visualization as the area matures, with the opportunity to take on increased responsibility as the function grows.

Location Denmark - Virum

Experience Minimum 3 Years

Department Data Engineering

How You Might Spend Your Days

As an Advanced Data Analyst, you’ll be:

  • Leading the development of Power BI dashboards, turning business needs into clear and impactful visualizations
  • Designing reusable BI templates and ensure consistency in structure, layout, KPIs, and naming
  • Working with data from the Fabric workspace and apply the right data access and integration approach
  • Developing and maintain DAX calculations to enable scalable and reliable reporting
  • Ensuring high quality, performance, and continuous improvement of dashboards while supporting the team in reusing solutions


Who You Are

We care about who you are as a person. In the end, how you work, and your energy is what impacts the results we achieve as a team. As a person, you are:

  • Thriving by taking ownership, leading tasks from start to finish independently
  • Great at understanding the ability to collaborate across business and IT
  • Structured and quality-conscious approach to solving tasks with attention to detail
  • Pragmatic and solution-oriented mindset, and motived by creating value to the line of business
  • Able to translate complex data into simple insights and act as a trusted sparring partner to the team


What You Bring To The Role

In all positions, there are some things that are preferred, and others, a bonus. If you do not meet all the qualifications listed below, you are welcome to offer alternative experience that you think would be valuable in this role:

  • Solid experience (3-5 years) within data analysis or Business Intelligence, combined with a relevant educational background in data, IT, engineering, or similar
  • Extensive experience in Power BI, including data modelling, DAX, and data visualization
  • Experience working with modern data platforms (e.g. Fabric, Azure, or similar) and building scalable BI solutions
  • Great understanding of data flows, data models, and hands-on experience with SQL and data processing
  • Fluency in English


Life at NNE

NNE is short for Novo Nordisk Engineering. And for all of us who work here, it means so much more.

At NNE, you become part of a vibrant, project-based environment built on close day-to-day collaboration. The role is therefore primarily on-site, with physical presence at the workplace expected five days a week. Limited flexibility for occasional remote work may be accommodated in line with business needs.

We are, at our core, a people business and, as such, committed to continuously developing our people and the way we work together. We do not expect you to know it all from day one, and we truly believe a great start makes a real difference. We therefore welcome new colleagues with our award-nominated onboarding programme, supported by a range of in-house talent and development initiatives designed to support your long-term growth.

You can read more about the benefits we offer here .

Did we spark your curiosity? If you’re ready to join our passion, let’s hear from you.

Deadline of application is 22th July 2026

Questions before applying? Send us a quick text to arrange a convenient time to chat. Contact Manager, Mayes Ali on +45 3075 7178 or Director, Niels Karim Høst-Madsen on +45 3075 2705.

Between week 28-30 you can expect a delay in reply due to summer holiday. Therefore, we encourage you to call before Monday 6 July if you have questions.

We kindly refrain from questions regarding the potential fit into the role - we encourage you to apply to the position even if you are a bit in doubt. You might just be the one we are looking for!

If you have any questions regarding the application process, please write to recruit@nne.com and we will assist you as soon as possible.

Please note:

  • We only accept applications submitted through our online recruitment system
  • You do not need to attach a cover letter, but please include a few sentences about why you are applying in your resume or CV
  • We invite to interviews on a running basis.


Fair hiring:

At NNE, we are committed to an inclusive recruitment process and equal opportunities for all job applicants. To maintain fairness, we request that you refrain from including a personal photo in your CV.

Still searching for the right opportunity?

If this position isn’t quite what you're looking for, but you know someone who would excel in this role, please share this job post. And don’t forget to explore our other exciting opportunities — your future role at NNE might be just around the corner! Visit our Career Site

4431238767,https://dk.linkedin.com/jobs/view/office-assistant-at-t%C3%BCv-s%C3%BCd-4431238767?position=10&pageNum=2&refId=9WBE1CWWBPgBIckuV3P5zg%3D%3D&trackingId=k2vHEdqyW28l5LNzaMJFnA%3D%3D,Office Assistant,TÜV SÜD,"Gentofte, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark","I TÜV SÜD brænder vi for teknologi. Innovationer påvirker vores dagligdag på utallige måder, og vi er dedikerede til at være en del af denne udvikling. Vi tester, auditerer, inspicerer og rådgiver. Vi holder aldrig op med at udfordre os selv for at sikre samfundets og menneskers sikkerhed. Vi lever og ånder for teknologi, stræber efter professionel ekspertise, og vi sætter vores præg. Vi tager fremtiden i egne hænder. Vi er TÜV SÜD.
OM JOBBET

Som Office Assistant bliver du en vigtig del af dagligdagen på kontoret. Du sørger for, at alt kører glat, og at dine kolleger har de bedste rammer for en god arbejdsdag. Du bliver en del af et levende og uformelt arbejdsmiljø, hvor der er fart på og plads til godt humør.
Jobbet deles med en anden person i tilsvarende stilling, og man hjælper hinanden ved sygdom og ferie, så kontoret altid er dækket ind.
Dine Opgaver Vil Blandt Andet Være

Assistere vores Administrative Coordinator og samarbejde med anden Office Assistant i den daglige vedligeholdelse af kontoret, hvilket betyder alle former for almindelig oprydning, opfyldning, bestilling af varer mv. der måtte være for at sikre at kontoret er optimalt som arbejdsplads. Derudover skal Medarbejderen sørge for madordningen på kontoret, Parkeringstilladelser og forefaldende ad hoc arbejde.
Medarbejderen kan blive sat til at løse andre opgaver efter aftale med nærmeste leder og som ligger inden for medarbejderens kompetencer.
Daglige kontoropgaver
Fylde op med kaffe, te, drikkevarer og snacks
Sætte frokost frem, rydde op efter frokost og rengøre alle bordoverflader
Klargøre mødelokaler med vand, kaffe og evt. forplejning
Hjælpe med let oprydning i fællesområder og mødelokaler
Gå med affald og sikre, at affaldssystemet fungerer i fællesområder
Service og ærinder internt og eksternt i samarbejde med Administrative Coordinator
Hjælpe med små praktiske opgaver fra Administrative Coordinator
Sørge for, at kontoret fremstår præsentabelt og indbydende
Håndtering af post og pakker i samarbejde med Administrative Coordinator
Vi Forventer, At Du
møder stabilt og til tiden
er serviceminded, hjælpsom og smilende
er frisk på at være fysisk aktiv og tage fat
har blik for, hvad der skal gøres – og gør det med et smil
trives i et uformelt og travlt miljø
Skal kunne kommunikere flydende på dansk
Bemærk: Du skal være fyldt 18 år for at kunne søge stillingen.
HVAD TILBYDER VI?
En arbejdsplads med højt humør, fart på og kolleger, der passer godt på hinanden
Fast mødetid mellem kl. 8.00 til kl. 15.00, bortset fra fredag, hvor der sluttes kl. 14.00
En fleksibel arbejdsordning med to dage den ene uge og tre dage den anden, svarende til ca. 18-20 timer i snit pr. uge.
Løn: kr.150,00 i timen
Gode erfaringer til dit CV – og mulighed for at blive en vigtig del af hverdagen i en større virksomhed
ANSØGNING OG KONTAKT
Vi ønsker stillingen besat pr. 1. juli 2026 eller snarest muligt.
Har du spørgsmål, er du velkommen til at kontakte vores Administration & Facilities Coordinator Camilla Fischer på admin.dk-BLS@tuvsud.com, hvortil ansøgning og CV også sendes.
HVEM ER VI?

Vi er en innovativ byggeteknisk TECH-virksomhed under TÜV SÜD, en global markedsleder inden for TIC med over 30.000 ansatte. Vi har 200 medarbejdere fordelt på vores afdelinger i København og Aarhus. Vi er et team bestående af ledelse, administration, IT, salg, magasinproduktion & marketing, storhuskonsulenter, tekniske assistenter samt udkørende konsulenter.

Vi er Danmarks førende virksomhed inden for besigtigelse af ejendomme og har en kultur præget af åben dialog, et stærkt fællesskab og en uformel tone med plads til humor. Vi vægter gensidig respekt, ansvarlighed og høj faglighed – og vi glæder os til at få dig med på holdet!

I TÜV SÜD har vi medarbejdere fra mere end 100 forskellige lande, der samarbejder. Mennesker med forskellige baggrunde, færdigheder og forskellige livsmål. Vores styrke kommer fra disse utallige og varierede perspektiver.

Vi er forpligtet til at være en inkluderende og mangfoldig arbejdsplads, hvor vi byder mennesker med alle baggrunde velkommen. Vi ønsker, at mangfoldighed og inklusion (D&I) skal være et fundament for vores virksomhed og skabe et miljø, hvor alle vores medarbejdere kan stole på, at de bliver behandlet med respekt, uanset køn, nationalitet, etnisk baggrund, tro, overbevisning, handicap, alder, seksuel orientering eller identitet. Derfor forventes det, at vores medarbejdere til enhver tid opfører sig i overensstemmelse med TÜV SÜD's etiske kodeks og virksomhedens værdier.
Vi er overbeviste om, at indlejring af D&I i kernen af vores arbejde i sig selv vil bidrage til TÜV SÜD's succes. Klik her for at læse mere om mangfoldighed hos TÜV SÜD.

4432272572,https://dk.linkedin.com/jobs/view/ungarbejder-7-timer-frederiksberg-at-lidl-danmark-4432272572?position=1&pageNum=5&refId=eGAo%2FKYkjEtDGncqSCzRrA%3D%3D&trackingId=yxI2O206XtdJAD2B5P%2B95A%3D%3D,Ungarbejder - 7 timer - Frederiksberg,Lidl Danmark,"Frederiksberg Municipality, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark","Introduktion
Er du under 18 år? Og drømmer du om at få et job, der giver lidt ekstra penge til dig selv hver måned? Så er du måske vores nye ungarbejder!
På #teamlidl bliver du en del af et seriøst godt sammenhold. Og som ungarbejder i Lidl får du et Lidl-tillæg på 5 kr. mere i timen.*
Mød din nye kollega Katrine

”Jeg er så glad for at arbejde i Lidl! Her er plads til alle, og alle bliver hørt. Vi har et rigtig godt team, hvor alle er gode til at hjælpe hinanden. Selve arbejdsmiljøet består af gode kunder, søde kollegaer og en flot butik,” fortæller Katrine, der er 17 år gammel og ungarbejder i Lidl.
Kan du bidrage til godt humør, fællesskab og en god service? Så er det måske dig, vi står og mangler!

På #teamlidl kommer du på hold med verdensklasse kollegaer. Når du starter, klæder vi dig på gennem et grundigt oplæringsforløb i vores koncepter. Vi går nemlig op i, at du lærer tingene rigtigt fra start, at du lykkes i dit job, og at du bidrager til et stærkt sammenhold med dine kollegaer.

Grænseløse karrieremulighederSom ungarbejder er der mange forskellige udviklingsmuligheder hos os. Har du f.eks. ambitioner om at gøre karriere i dagligvarebranchen? Så er jobbet som ungarbejder det perfekte sted at starte. Din erfaring som ungarbejder vil nemlig kun være en fordel, hvis du senere ønsker at søge ind på vores elevuddannelse, som vi mener, er Danmarks suverænt bedste! Det er ligeledes en stor fordel at have butikserfaring, hvis du drømmer om at arbejde på vores hovedkontor.
Ift. den overenskomstmæssig takst pr. marts 2025.
Om jobbet

Som ungarbejder hos os skal du, først og fremmest, hjælpe dine kollegaer, så vores kunder altid får en god indkøbsoplevelse i butikken. Du ved, at du har gjort dit arbejde godt, når du har ydet en god service – og det gør dig glad! Dine opgaver er blandt andet at:
Give vores kunder en god oplevelse i butikken sammen med dine kollegaer
Få butikken til at shine, ved at fylde op og trimme hylderne så de altid står snorlige
Få varerne scannet hurtigt gennem kasseapparatet med et smil på læben
Svinge moppen, når gulvet er beskidt
Om dig
Når du starter i Lidl, får du en grundig oplæring i butikken. Derfor er det ikke afgørende for os, at du har erfaring, men vi forventer, at du:
Er under 18 år, dog ikke yngre end 15 år
Har afsluttet 9. klasse
Er smilende og har masser af god energi
Har gåpåmod, klør på og vægter teamspirit højt
Møder til tiden og er interesseret i at gøre dit arbejde godt
Har mulighed for at arbejde eftermiddag, aften og i weekenden
Vi tilbyder
Udover at du får verdensklasse kollegaer, får du som ungarbejder også:
Et Lidl-tillæg på 5 kr. mere i timen*
Et udbetalt tillæg i henhold til overenskomsten, når du arbejder på skæve tidspunkter - fx aftener, weekender og helligdage
Løn og vilkår efter gældende overenskomst med HK
Erfaring med at samarbejde i et team og erfaring med service - hvilket vil give dig en stor fordel i dit videre arbejdsliv
Fleksible arbejdstider, så du let kan kombinere jobbet med skole og fritidsaktiviteter
Et spændende og varieret job med fart over feltet!
Gode karrieremuligheder, hvor du får forudsætningerne for at lykkes
Rabatordning hos flere udbydere af rejser, shopping, kultur, fitness mm.
Vil du med på #teamlidl?
Nu ved du forhåbentlig endnu mere om #teamlidl og jobbet som ungarbejder, end du gjorde i forvejen! Er jobbet som ungarbejder i Lidl noget for dig? Så send dit motiverede CV allerede i dag!
Sådan søger du stillingen:
Klik på ”Søg nu”
Udfyld formularen
Vedhæft dit motiverede CV
Vi rekrutterer løbende, så send dit motiverede CV hurtigst muligt. Da butikken er åben alle ugens dage, vil der forekomme dag-, aften- og weekendarbejde.

Har du spørgsmål til stillingen eller ansøgningsprocessen, er du velkommen til at kontakte Recruiting på jobilidloest@lidl.dk eller tlf. 7635 0635, mandag – torsdag 8.00 – 15.00/ fredag 08.00 – 14.00.
Vi ser frem til at modtage dit CV!

Introduktion

Er du under 18 år? Og drømmer du om at få et job, der giver lidt ekstra penge til dig selv hver måned? Så er du måske vores nye ungarbejder!

På #teamlidl bliver du en del af et seriøst godt sammenhold. Og som ungarbejder i Lidl får du et Lidl-tillæg på 5 kr. mere i timen.*

Mød din nye kollega Katrine

”Jeg er så glad for at arbejde i Lidl! Her er plads til alle, og alle bliver hørt. Vi har et rigtig godt team, hvor alle er gode til at hjælpe hinanden. Selve arbejdsmiljøet består af gode kunder, søde kollegaer og en flot butik,” fortæller Katrine, der er 17 år gammel og ungarbejder i Lidl.

Kan du bidrage til godt humør, fællesskab og en god service? Så er det måske dig, vi står og mangler!

På #teamlidl kommer du på hold med verdensklasse kollegaer. Når du starter, klæder vi dig på gennem et grundigt oplæringsforløb i vores koncepter. Vi går nemlig op i, at du lærer tingene rigtigt fra start, at du lykkes i dit job, og at du bidrager til et stærkt sammenhold med dine kollegaer.

Grænseløse karrieremulighederSom ungarbejder er der mange forskellige udviklingsmuligheder hos os. Har du f.eks. ambitioner om at gøre karriere i dagligvarebranchen? Så er jobbet som ungarbejder det perfekte sted at starte. Din erfaring som ungarbejder vil nemlig kun være en fordel, hvis du senere ønsker at søge ind på vores elevuddannelse, som vi mener, er Danmarks suverænt bedste! Det er ligeledes en stor fordel at have butikserfaring, hvis du drømmer om at arbejde på vores hovedkontor.

  • Ift. den overenskomstmæssig takst pr. marts 2025.

Om jobbet

Som ungarbejder hos os skal du, først og fremmest, hjælpe dine kollegaer, så vores kunder altid får en god indkøbsoplevelse i butikken. Du ved, at du har gjort dit arbejde godt, når du har ydet en god service – og det gør dig glad! Dine opgaver er blandt andet at:

  • Give vores kunder en god oplevelse i butikken sammen med dine kollegaer
  • Få butikken til at shine, ved at fylde op og trimme hylderne så de altid står snorlige
  • Få varerne scannet hurtigt gennem kasseapparatet med et smil på læben
  • Svinge moppen, når gulvet er beskidt

Om dig

Når du starter i Lidl, får du en grundig oplæring i butikken. Derfor er det ikke afgørende for os, at du har erfaring, men vi forventer, at du:

  • Er under 18 år, dog ikke yngre end 15 år
  • Har afsluttet 9. klasse
  • Er smilende og har masser af god energi
  • Har gåpåmod, klør på og vægter teamspirit højt
  • Møder til tiden og er interesseret i at gøre dit arbejde godt
  • Har mulighed for at arbejde eftermiddag, aften og i weekenden

Vi tilbyder

Udover at du får verdensklasse kollegaer, får du som ungarbejder også:

  • Et Lidl-tillæg på 5 kr. mere i timen*
  • Et udbetalt tillæg i henhold til overenskomsten, når du arbejder på skæve tidspunkter - fx aftener, weekender og helligdage
  • Løn og vilkår efter gældende overenskomst med HK
  • Erfaring med at samarbejde i et team og erfaring med service - hvilket vil give dig en stor fordel i dit videre arbejdsliv
  • Fleksible arbejdstider, så du let kan kombinere jobbet med skole og fritidsaktiviteter
  • Et spændende og varieret job med fart over feltet!
  • Gode karrieremuligheder, hvor du får forudsætningerne for at lykkes
  • Rabatordning hos flere udbydere af rejser, shopping, kultur, fitness mm.

Vil du med på #teamlidl?

Nu ved du forhåbentlig endnu mere om #teamlidl og jobbet som ungarbejder, end du gjorde i forvejen! Er jobbet som ungarbejder i Lidl noget for dig? Så send dit motiverede CV allerede i dag!

Sådan søger du stillingen:

  • Klik på ”Søg nu”
  • Udfyld formularen
  • Vedhæft dit motiverede CV

Vi rekrutterer løbende, så send dit motiverede CV hurtigst muligt. Da butikken er åben alle ugens dage, vil der forekomme dag-, aften- og weekendarbejde.

Har du spørgsmål til stillingen eller ansøgningsprocessen, er du velkommen til at kontakte Recruiting på jobilidloest@lidl.dk eller tlf. 7635 0635, mandag – torsdag 8.00 – 15.00/ fredag 08.00 – 14.00.

Vi ser frem til at modtage dit CV!

4428132871,https://dk.linkedin.com/jobs/view/listening-bar-brewery-internship-at-kombucha-inc-4428132871?position=7&pageNum=5&refId=eGAo%2FKYkjEtDGncqSCzRrA%3D%3D&trackingId=gawSZBc1hGvsJE8bApTDDA%3D%3D,Listening Bar & Brewery Internship,Kombucha Inc.,"Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark","Internship: Help Build and Run Our New Listening Bar Concept 6–12 months | Copenhagen | €700/month + possible paid shifts
The Opportunity
We’re building something new in the heart of Kødbyen — a small, ambitious listening bar located right next to the Kombucha Inc. brewery.

The bar is already open, but we’re still shaping the space, the concept, the routines, the events, the atmosphere, and the way people experience it. That means there is a lot to build — and we’re looking for a hands-on intern who wants to be part of that journey from the inside.

If you’re curious about hospitality, music, events, branding, community-building, social media, and what it takes to turn a small bar into a living concept, this is your chance to get involved in almost every part of the process.
This internship is especially relevant for a student who needs a practical internship as part of their study programme.
Your Impact
Building the Bar & Concept
Be part of developing the bar’s identity, atmosphere, routines, and guest experience.
Help shape how the bar looks, feels, communicates, and operates day to day.
Support the team in turning ideas into practical systems, events, and experiences.
Contribute to building a place that feels welcoming, creative, and alive.
Music, Events & Community
Help plan, coordinate, and run events at the bar.
Support everything from listening sessions and DJ nights to tastings, community gatherings, and creative collaborations.
Assist with booking, planning, communication, setup, execution, and follow-up.
Bring ideas for new events, music formats, partnerships, and ways to activate the space.
Operations & Running the Place
Take part in the daily running of the bar, including opening routines, service preparation, stock, and general coordination.
Help improve workflows, systems, and routines as the bar develops.
Work closely with the team to make sure the place runs smoothly, while still leaving room for experimentation and creativity.
There may also be opportunities to take paid shifts in the bar alongside the internship.
Social Media & Communication
Help build the bar’s voice and presence on social media.
Create content around the space, events, drinks, music, people, and behind-the-scenes moments.
Support with posts, stories, event communication, newsletters, and simple campaigns.
Help us tell the story of a new listening bar being built in real time.
Business & Concept Development
Support the development of new ideas for drinks, events, collaborations, and guest experiences.
Research trends, local culture, relevant brands, potential partners, music communities, and new opportunities.
Help us understand what works, what people respond to, and how the bar can grow.
What’s In It For You
A front-row seat to building a listening bar and hospitality concept from the ground up.
Hands-on experience across events, operations, branding, social media, music programming, and community-building.
A startup environment where your ideas can become real quickly.
The chance to work closely with a small, creative, and ambitious team.
Experience that is directly relevant if you’re interested in hospitality, entrepreneurship, culture, events, branding, music, or food and beverage.
A monthly internship compensation of €700.
Possible paid shifts in the bar, depending on availability and fit.
Who We’re Looking For
Someone curious, hands-on, and excited about building things.
A student looking for a 6–12 month internship as part of their study programme.
Someone interested in hospitality, music, events, social media, culture, branding, or entrepreneurship.
A person who enjoys working with people and creating good experiences.
Someone organized enough to help make things happen, but flexible enough to work in a place that is still being built.
A collaborative mindset — we’re a small team, and we work closely together.
You don’t need to know everything already. But you should be ready to learn, take initiative, and roll up your sleeves.
How To Jump In
No need for a lengthy application. Just send us:
A short video introducing yourself, or
Your CV, and examples of relevant past work, projects, events, content, or ideas.
Let us know why you want to be part of building the listening bar — and what you would love to help create.
Note: This is an internship with a monthly compensation of €700. There may also be the possibility of taking paid shifts in the bar.

4424715283,https://dk.linkedin.com/jobs/view/applied-ai-internship-at-juris-legal-4424715283?position=10&pageNum=5&refId=eGAo%2FKYkjEtDGncqSCzRrA%3D%3D&trackingId=25HPl5XCtI%2FbAe1RW8FQ5Q%3D%3D,Applied AI Internship,Juris Legal,"Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark","We are Juris Legal, and we are building an AI product for the legal world. To build the best product, we also need to be the best at using and applying AI in our own work.

We are looking for an intern who gets genuinely excited about new AI tools and what is happening right now, and who also has the discipline to test things properly and turn good ideas into reliable, real workflows. This is a hands-on role where you will explore, evaluate, and implement tools in day-to-day processes.

You will be the kind of person who is happy to try something new like OpenClaw the moment it appears, but you also care about whether it actually works, how we measure it, and how we roll it out so the team can trust it.
What You Will Do
Stay on top of new AI tools and workflows and actively test what is relevant for us (including things like OpenClaw and other emerging tools)
Bring the team the best new ideas, while keeping a clear and structured approach to what we try and why
Apply tools to real internal processes so we work faster and with higher quality
Build and improve small prototypes and utilities (scripts, lightweight apps, automations, templates, internal tools)
Turn experiments into repeatable workflows through short documentation, playbooks, and simple training sessions
Evaluate tools with good judgment and clear criteria: quality, reliability, cost, privacy, and security
Work closely with the team building the product and support how we operate day to day
What We Are Looking For
You do not need a specific title or background. You could be studying law, business, computer science, engineering, design, or something else entirely.
What Matters Most Is That You
Get excited about new tools and love learning fast
Balance curiosity with a practical mindset and a structured approach to implementation
Like building useful things and iterating quickly
Communicate clearly in writing and in short demos
Can separate what is valuable from what is hype
Take ownership and are comfortable working with real workflows and real constraints
Technical experience can show up in different ways. Any of these can be a strong fit:
Coding (Python, JavaScript, TypeScript, or similar)
Working with APIs and automation
Prototyping workflows and connecting tools in a practical way
What Success Looks Like
By the end of the internship, you have helped us:
Level up how we use AI in daily work
Ship a few useful prototypes or workflows that we actually keep using
Create clear guidance so the whole team benefits
Make smart recommendations on what to adopt, pilot, or avoid
What You Will Get
High autonomy and real ownership
Direct collaboration with the people building the product
A front-row seat in legal AI and the chance to shape how we work
A role where your work has visible impact inside the company

We are Juris Legal, and we are building an AI product for the legal world. To build the best product, we also need to be the best at using and applying AI in our own work.

We are looking for an intern who gets genuinely excited about new AI tools and what is happening right now, and who also has the discipline to test things properly and turn good ideas into reliable, real workflows. This is a hands-on role where you will explore, evaluate, and implement tools in day-to-day processes.

You will be the kind of person who is happy to try something new like OpenClaw the moment it appears, but you also care about whether it actually works, how we measure it, and how we roll it out so the team can trust it.

What You Will Do

  • Stay on top of new AI tools and workflows and actively test what is relevant for us (including things like OpenClaw and other emerging tools)
  • Bring the team the best new ideas, while keeping a clear and structured approach to what we try and why
  • Apply tools to real internal processes so we work faster and with higher quality
  • Build and improve small prototypes and utilities (scripts, lightweight apps, automations, templates, internal tools)
  • Turn experiments into repeatable workflows through short documentation, playbooks, and simple training sessions
  • Evaluate tools with good judgment and clear criteria: quality, reliability, cost, privacy, and security
  • Work closely with the team building the product and support how we operate day to day

What We Are Looking For

You do not need a specific title or background. You could be studying law, business, computer science, engineering, design, or something else entirely.

What Matters Most Is That You

  • Get excited about new tools and love learning fast
  • Balance curiosity with a practical mindset and a structured approach to implementation
  • Like building useful things and iterating quickly
  • Communicate clearly in writing and in short demos
  • Can separate what is valuable from what is hype
  • Take ownership and are comfortable working with real workflows and real constraints

Technical experience can show up in different ways. Any of these can be a strong fit:

  • Coding (Python, JavaScript, TypeScript, or similar)
  • Working with APIs and automation
  • Prototyping workflows and connecting tools in a practical way

What Success Looks Like

By the end of the internship, you have helped us:

  • Level up how we use AI in daily work
  • Ship a few useful prototypes or workflows that we actually keep using
  • Create clear guidance so the whole team benefits
  • Make smart recommendations on what to adopt, pilot, or avoid

What You Will Get

  • High autonomy and real ownership
  • Direct collaboration with the people building the product
  • A front-row seat in legal AI and the chance to shape how we work
  • A role where your work has visible impact inside the company

4432789639,https://dk.linkedin.com/jobs/view/data-engineer-at-tryg-4432789639?position=1&pageNum=7&refId=WOfvOLlvdv8fzmBtazqq1w%3D%3D&trackingId=TXkpZBzJjUu9P7tVdkOb9g%3D%3D,Data Engineer,Tryg,"Ballerup, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark","Join the Customer Excellence domain at Tryg and help shape the future of data-driven customer engagement. As a Data Engineer, you will build scalable data pipelines, work with modern technologies such as Databricks, Kafka, and Python, and turn complex data into trusted, business-critical assets. If you are passionate about data engineering, cloud platforms, and solving technical challenges in a collaborative environment, this is an opportunity to make a real impact.
About the job | Data Engineer

Data is one of the most valuable assets for Tryg, and customer engagement is one of the key strategic pillars for our entire business. You will work at the intersection of both as you will transform data into actionable insights that drive meaningful customer experiences.
Data engineering plays a critical role in our data journey, as we integrate with ever more systems/sources, implement streaming, and standardize our raw data assets through modelling.

Customer engagement data is scattered across a multitude of different systems, and to work with customer data and processes at scale, we have built a modern cloud native foundation based on a combination of open source and proprietary software to enable easy, purpose-driven, and compliant access to data from those systems.

In your role as our new Data Engineer, you will work with cutting-edge tools and technologies on building and maintaining data pipelines for the Customer Excellence domain sources and applications on the data platform. You will be given responsibility for tasks such as:
Deconstruct and “understand” data from source systems through an end-to-end ETL process
Design, development, and maintenance of data pipelines, as well as CI/CD processes for data workflows and infrastructure
Development of foundational application components
Coordinate efforts of the extended team of both in-house and external developers, and follow up on technical/infrastructure changes with our Platform team
Ensure code quality across all relevant applications, including compliance with established DevOps principles
Coordinate ingestion of raw data from new streaming, batch, and API-based data sources
Development of AI and ML components for internal use cases
Ensure data quality, integrity, and governance
Work on custom integrations to and from both 1st and 3rd party systems

As a data engineer at Tryg, you will mainly be working with technologies such as SQLMesh, Databricks, Java, Python, Kafka, Kubernetes, Docker, Redis, GitLab CI/CD, REST APIs, and we expect you to be familiar with at least some of them.
About you | Curious, proactive, and passionate about data engineering
To be successful in the job, we expect you to have a relevant education in software engineering, data engineering, data science, data management, or similar.

Our data engineering tech stack is heavily focused around Databricks, Kafka, and big data processing. It is a definite advantage if you can demonstrate experience from that area, but experience as a software engineer or data scientist, genuine curiosity, and a desire to invest in your own development can compensate for a lack of hands-on experience. If you have only worked with Java or Python development and batch processing but want to expand your skillset with streaming – we'd still love to hear from you!

We are looking for someone who takes the initiative to solve problems and get things done. You see technical challenges as an exciting new obstacle to overcome, and a reason to improve your skills. To succeed in the role, you must be self-driven and strive to challenge the status quo.
Our teams are international, so you communicate clearly in English, and preferably also in a Scandinavian language, both verbally and in writing.
About us | The Customer Excellence domain

You will be part of the Customer Excellence domain in Tryg's CDO division. The area has Nordic responsibility for managing, supporting, and creating business value from customer engagement data in Tryg Group, from data modelling and integrations to compliance and vendor relations.
Together with awesome colleagues, you will contribute directly to Tryg’s “United Towards ’27” strategy and make an impact on both internal and customer-facing applications.
We look forward to hearing from you!
Send your CV and application as soon as possible, but no later than 26 July 2026. We will be conducting interviews starting in August. We encourage you not to include a photo in your CV.
We review applications on an ongoing basis and close the process once the right candidate is identified.
For questions, please contact Head of CX Technology, Pavel Kruglov at pavel.kruglov@tryg.no

If you are passionate about data engineering, cloud platforms, and solving technical challenges in a collaborative environment, this is an opportunity to make a real impact.

About the job | Data Engineer

Data is one of the most valuable assets for Tryg, and customer engagement is one of the key strategic pillars for our entire business. You will work at the intersection of both as you will transform data into actionable insights that drive meaningful customer experiences.

Data engineering plays a critical role in our data journey, as we integrate with ever more systems/sources, implement streaming, and standardize our raw data assets through modelling.

Customer engagement data is scattered across a multitude of different systems, and to work with customer data and processes at scale, we have built a modern cloud native foundation based on a combination of open source and proprietary software to enable easy, purpose-driven, and compliant access to data from those systems.

In your role as our new Data Engineer, you will work with cutting-edge tools and technologies on building and maintaining data pipelines for the Customer Excellence domain sources and applications on the data platform. You will be given responsibility for tasks such as:

  • Deconstruct and “understand” data from source systems through an end-to-end ETL process
  • Design, development, and maintenance of data pipelines, as well as CI/CD processes for data workflows and infrastructure
  • Development of foundational application components
  • Coordinate efforts of the extended team of both in-house and external developers, and follow up on technical/infrastructure changes with our Platform team
  • Ensure code quality across all relevant applications, including compliance with established DevOps principles
  • Coordinate ingestion of raw data from new streaming, batch, and API-based data sources
  • Development of AI and ML components for internal use cases
  • Ensure data quality, integrity, and governance
  • Work on custom integrations to and from both 1st and 3rd party systems

As a data engineer at Tryg, you will mainly be working with technologies such as SQLMesh, Databricks, Java, Python, Kafka, Kubernetes, Docker, Redis, GitLab CI/CD, REST APIs, and we expect you to be familiar with at least some of them.

About you | Curious, proactive, and passionate about data engineering

To be successful in the job, we expect you to have a relevant education in software engineering, data engineering, data science, data management, or similar.

Our data engineering tech stack is heavily focused around Databricks, Kafka, and big data processing. We are looking for someone who takes the initiative to solve problems and get things done. You see technical challenges as an exciting new obstacle to overcome, and a reason to improve your skills. To succeed in the role, you must be self-driven and strive to challenge the status quo.

Our teams are international, so you communicate clearly in English, and preferably also in a Scandinavian language, both verbally and in writing.

About us | The Customer Excellence domain

You will be part of the Customer Excellence domain in Tryg's CDO division. The area has Nordic responsibility for managing, supporting, and creating business value from customer engagement data in Tryg Group, from data modelling and integrations to compliance and vendor relations.

Together with awesome colleagues, you will contribute directly to Tryg’s “United Towards ’27” strategy and make an impact on both internal and customer-facing applications.

We look forward to hearing from you!

Send your CV and application as soon as possible, but no later than 26 July 2026. We will be conducting interviews starting in August. We encourage you not to include a photo in your CV.

We review applications on an ongoing basis and close the process once the right candidate is identified.

For questions, please contact Head of CX Technology, Pavel Kruglov at pavel.kruglov@tryg.no

4432667016,https://dk.linkedin.com/jobs/view/business-intelligence-specialist-at-boozt-4432667016?position=4&pageNum=7&refId=WOfvOLlvdv8fzmBtazqq1w%3D%3D&trackingId=NsG57%2FLbLtvPx448cPuy5w%3D%3D,Business Intelligence Specialist,Boozt,"Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark","THE OPPORTUNITY
As the leading Nordic online department store - 1,600+ brands, 14 markets, one of the world's most automated fulfilment centres - data powers every decision we make at Boozt.
We're hiring two new colleagues across our BI function: one in our Logistics Reporting & Analytics (Warehouse & operations) and in our Commercial Business Partner Team.
Different teams, different challenges, one thing in common: your data insights help shape smarter decisions and turn them into real business impact.
Logistics Reporting & Analytics: Early Career
Operations-focused

Learn the business from the ground up. Every order that reaches a customer is the result of an efficient supply chain, a distribution network, and a customer service operation working in sync. You'll sit close to the warehouse floor, fulfilment, returns, and last mile, turning operational data into dashboards and analyses that help teams move faster and smarter.
WHAT YOU WILL DO
Build, develop, and maintain QlikCloud apps end to end - from data load and modelling to the dashboards teams use every day
Write and optimise load scripts, build data models, and keep apps fast and reliable as data volumes grow
Run ad hoc analysis in Qlik to answer operational questions quickly when they come up
Turn requirements from distribution, supply chain, and customer service teams into well-built, reusable reporting
Maintain and improve our existing Qlik reporting - refactoring, fixing, and extending what's already there
Work with our data and engineering teams on data quality and the pipelines that feed Qlik (BigQuery)
YOUR PROFILE
2> years in a BI, analytics data role - internships, thesis work, and student jobs count
Comfortable with SQL and Qlik scripting / data modelling, or genuinely motivated to get there fast
Hands-on with QlikCloud, or able to learn it quickly (experience with Power BI, Tableau, or Looker transfers well
column2: we use Qlik with BigQuery)

column1: You move from ""here's the data"" to ""here's what we should build""
Curious about how the business actually works, not just the numbers it produces
Commercial Business Partner Team: Mid-level
Engineering-focused

Work with large and complex datasets to build the data foundation behind key commercial decisions. 4427831812,https://dk.linkedin.com/jobs/view/data-management-consultant-at-netcompany-4427831812?position=6&pageNum=7&refId=WOfvOLlvdv8fzmBtazqq1w%3D%3D&trackingId=99FSOKjluB51b44RGs4J%2FA%3D%3D,Data Management Consultant,Netcompany,"Aarhus, Central Denmark Region, Denmark","Job Description

Are you passionate about data and driven by the opportunity to make a real impact by helping companies turn facts into actionable insights? Do you thrive in dynamic environments where you work with some of the most complex and modern data solutions? If so, you might be our next colleague.
Responsibilities & Technologies
In Data Management, you will become part of a team that contributes to enabling our customers to develop and manage their business based on data and knowledge.

You will work in the intersection of technology and business. Information and data are at the core of our work, and we can see that our analyses, advice, and final solutions create value for our customers. The work involves developing technical solutions to collect, process, model, analyze, and visualize data.

To provide the best possible advice and support to our customers, we place great importance on staying up to date with the latest trends and technologies in data management. You will work with a broad range of market-leading technologies, including: Azure SQL Server, Snowflake, dbt, Microsoft SQL Server, and others.
Your tasks are highly varied and depend on your skills and the direction you wish to develop.
Your Responsibilites Will Typically Include
Design, modeling, and building of data structures such as databases.
Structuring and collection of data from various sources.
Implementation of business logic on data for use in reporting.
Customer advisory, strategy work, and business analysis in collaboration with our customers.
Development of reports for business users with a focus on front-end.
About You

You are newly graduated or have up to a couple of years of relevant professional experience within data management. Through your education, you have gained a solid professional foundation and a logical approach to handling complex data and challenges. You thrive in a development-oriented environment with a focus on collaboration and continuous personal and professional development.
To succeed in this role, you have experience with or the interest to learn:
ETL and Database technologies.
Languages such as various versions of SQL, Python, and R.
Business Intelligence tools: Power BI, Tableau, and others.
Full professional proficiency in Danish and English (written and spoken) is a must.
Continue developing with us
At Netcompany, we focus on your personal and professional development.

When you join us, you will be assigned a personal mentor who will guide you through your development and help you achieve your goals. The close relationship with an experienced colleague helps ensure the well-being of each employee and provides you with concrete insights into the many different career opportunities as you build your experience.

Additionally, you will be enrolled in our internal training programme, Netcompany Academy, where you are offered courses and seminars on everything from technologies to customer management. All courses are taught by experienced Netcompany colleagues, ensuring that all content is highly relevant and builds upon your professional skills, preparing you for various tasks and continuously improving your expertise throughout your career with us.
Join an inclusive community

At Netcompany, we highly prioritise an inclusive community. We actively promote an open and welcoming work culture based on collaboration and mutual trust, where there is always an opportunity for sparring.

As part of our culture and values, all employees, regardless of background, identity, and experience, are met with understanding and respect and given equal opportunities for development and well-being.

As we are an international company with offices in many countries, you will have the opportunity to learn and collaborate internationally with colleagues from across the world. We offer a shared environment where we engage with each other across departments and projects. Additionally, we have a wide range of social and professional events that provide you with the best opportunities to expand and build a network early in your career.
Send us your application
Upload your resume and a motivational letter to our recruitment system via the link. If you have unanswered questions, feel free to write to hr.dk@netcompany.com.

All qualified candidates are encouraged to apply regardless of gender, gender expression, sexuality, disability, age, religion and belief, political views, nationality, ethnicity, or social background.

We are committed to creating an inclusive and diverse environment. Our recruitment processes are based on individual merit. If you require any reasonable adjustments or additional support during the interview process, please email our recruiters at reasonableadjustments@netcompany.com for assistance.

4436348938,https://dk.linkedin.com/jobs/view/bi-lead-data-analytics-enablement-at-implement-consulting-group-4436348938?position=7&pageNum=7&refId=WOfvOLlvdv8fzmBtazqq1w%3D%3D&trackingId=hGnEsUyaWRfP9DR4lgiMkQ%3D%3D,BI Lead - Data & Analytics Enablement,Implement Consulting Group,"Gentofte, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark","We’re looking for a BI Lead to take ownership of how we structure, deliver, and scale analytics across the organisation.
What you'll do

In this role, you will lead our Business Intelligence capability, ensuring that data is transformed into clear insights, strong decision support, and business impact. You will operate at the intersection of analytics, data modelling, and business, and play a key role in shaping how we work with data across teams.
As our new BI Lead, you will:
Own and evolve our BI capability
Define and drive the overall approach to Business Intelligence
Establish standards for data models, reporting structures, KPI definitions, and data usability
Ensure consistency across reports, dashboards, and datasets
Shape how AI and agentic capabilities are applied within BI — setting direction on where they add value
Lead analytics, reporting, and data modelling
Own and evolve core datasets, Power BI and Power Apps solutions
Take ownership of how data is structured, modelled, and exposed for reporting
Raise the bar for how insights are communicated and used
Act as senior business partner
Work closely with senior stakeholders to shape key questions and analyses
Challenge assumptions and bring analytical rigour into discussions
Enable teams to become more data-driven in practice
Drive BI maturity and adoption
Translate business needs into analytical structures and data requirements
Work with data engineers to prioritise and improve the data foundation
Identify and lead initiatives that increase adoption and value of BI across the organization
Increase adoption and value of BI and AI-enabled analytics across the organisation
What you'll bring

We're looking for someone who combines analytical leadership, strong execution, and stakeholder impact – with proven experience in Business Intelligence, analytics, and data-driven decision support, and the ability to own BI solutions end-to-end.
You'll bring:
a solid technical foundation
– you have strong experience with Power BI, semantic models and SQL, as well as a solid understanding of data modelling, dataset design, and analytical structures
a leadership profile
– you take ownership beyond individual deliverables and set direction, not just execution
column2: you communicate complexity clearly and engage confidently with senior stakeholders
column3: you drive progress in an environment without rigid structure

column1: a curiosity beyond the core
– a genuine interest in how AI can extend BI, and ideally some exposure to Power Apps or other low-code tools
What we offer

This is neither a pure developer role nor a pure platform ownership role – and that's what makes it interesting. You'll own how BI works as a capability, act as the link between data foundation and business decisions and help shape how AI and agentic solutions become a natural part of how BI operates at Implement.

At Implement, you'll find few rules and guidelines, and that's on purpose. We aim to create the right conditions for talented people to do their best work, with a great deal of autonomy and a strong culture of learning, sparring, and peer development. You'll be part of an ambitious internal team where your work has direct and visible impact.
You'll know you're succeeding when reporting is trusted across the organisation, stakeholders reach for data before making decisions, and analytical discussions get sharper over time.
About Implement

Implement Consulting Group (founded 1996) is an international management consulting firm with over 1,800 employees. Headquartered in Copenhagen, we have 12 offices across the Nordics, the DACH region, and the United States, making us one of the largest European consultancies. We support organisations in delivering sustainable strategic, commercial, and operational transformations. To help our clients succeed with their most critical change initiatives, we aim to attract, develop, and promote the best talent by creating a unique, collaborative learning environment where people can follow their passion, shape their own careers, and choose their own leaders.
The application deadline is 10 August
. Interviews will be held during August, and we look forward to being in touch with shortlisted candidates shortly after the deadline.
We look forward to hearing from you!

4424864118,https://dk.linkedin.com/jobs/view/it-supporter-at-mansoft-4424864118?position=1&pageNum=10&refId=pmzt1fCmv9B0JzIQPBV3bw%3D%3D&trackingId=OOeMFYNoAn4UUOpBoANpNw%3D%3D,IT-supporter,Mansoft,"Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark","Vil du arbejde tæt på brugerne, lære forskellige IT-miljøer at kende og udvikle dig som IT-supporter? Så er rollen som IT-supportkonsulent hos Mansoft måske noget for dig.
Om os
Mansoft er et IT-konsulenthus, der har eksisteret siden 2004 med kontorer i Holte og Hinnerup.
Vores fastansatte konsulenter arbejder inden for support, infrastruktur og endpoint management, hvor vi hjælper både offentlige og private kunder med opgaver, drift og projekter.
Rollen

Som IT-supportkonsulent vil du arbejde ude hos kunderne, typisk i længere perioder, og blive en del af det interne supporteam. Det giver dig mulighed for at fordybe dig i kundens miljø, lære brugerne at kende og blive en naturlig del af deres hverdag.
Kunderne varierer i både branche, systemer og arbejdsgange, hvilket giver bred erfaring med forskellige IT-miljøer og måder at arbejde med support på.
Eksempler på supportopgaver:
Daglig telefonisk, walk-in og onsite support
Support og udrulning af applikationer og operativsystemer
Klargøring, installation og fejlfinding på arbejdsstationer
Support og konfiguration af printere og mobile enheder
Opsætning og support af mødelokaler
Brugeradministration samt on/offboarding
Dokumentation og opfølgning i ticketsystemer
Vi forestiller os, at du:
Er uddannet IT-supporter
Kommunikerer forståeligt på både dansk og engelsk
Har minimum et par års erfaring med både 1st og 2nd level support
Arbejder dagligt med følgende teknologier:
Active Directory / Entra ID
Microsoft 365 (Outlook, Teams, OneDrive, SharePoint)
Windows 10/11
iOS og Android
Intune
ServiceNow eller lignende ticketsystem
Vores kunder er primært baseret i Storkøbenhavn og tilgængelige med offentlig transport. Du behøver derfor ikke have kørekort eller egen bil for at kunne varetage rollen.
Kan du se dig selv i dette?
Du møder brugerne med et smil og en positiv tilgang som skaber tryghed
Du kommunikerer på en enkel og forståelig måde
Du tager ejerskab for dine sager og følger dem helt til dørs
Du tager naturligt initiativ til nye opgaver og hjælper, hvor der er behov
Hvis du kan svare ja, så vil du passe godt ind i rollen og i måden, vi arbejder med support hos vores kunder.
Vores løfte til dig

Vil du arbejde tæt på brugerne, lære forskellige IT-miljøer at kende og udvikle dig som IT-supporter? Så er rollen som IT-supportkonsulent hos Mansoft måske noget for dig.


Om os

Mansoft er et IT-konsulenthus, der har eksisteret siden 2004 med kontorer i Holte og Hinnerup.

Vores fastansatte konsulenter arbejder inden for support, infrastruktur og endpoint management, hvor vi hjælper både offentlige og private kunder med opgaver, drift og projekter.


Rollen

Som IT-supportkonsulent vil du arbejde ude hos kunderne, typisk i længere perioder, og blive en del af det interne supporteam. Det giver dig mulighed for at fordybe dig i kundens miljø, lære brugerne at kende og blive en naturlig del af deres hverdag.


Kunderne varierer i både branche, systemer og arbejdsgange, hvilket giver bred erfaring med forskellige IT-miljøer og måder at arbejde med support på.


Eksempler på supportopgaver:

  • Daglig telefonisk, walk-in og onsite support
  • Support og udrulning af applikationer og operativsystemer
  • Klargøring, installation og fejlfinding på arbejdsstationer
  • Support og konfiguration af printere og mobile enheder
  • Opsætning og support af mødelokaler
  • Brugeradministration samt on/offboarding
  • Dokumentation og opfølgning i ticketsystemer


Vi forestiller os, at du:

  • Er uddannet IT-supporter
  • Kommunikerer forståeligt på både dansk og engelsk
  • Har minimum et par års erfaring med både 1st og 2nd level support


Arbejder dagligt med følgende teknologier:

  • Active Directory / Entra ID
  • Microsoft 365 (Outlook, Teams, OneDrive, SharePoint)
  • Windows 10/11
  • iOS og Android
  • Intune
  • ServiceNow eller lignende ticketsystem


Vores kunder er primært baseret i Storkøbenhavn og tilgængelige med offentlig transport. Du behøver derfor ikke have kørekort eller egen bil for at kunne varetage rollen.


Kan du se dig selv i dette?

  • Du møder brugerne med et smil og en positiv tilgang som skaber tryghed
  • Du kommunikerer på en enkel og forståelig måde
  • Du tager ejerskab for dine sager og følger dem helt til dørs
  • Du tager naturligt initiativ til nye opgaver og hjælper, hvor der er behov


Hvis du kan svare ja, så vil du passe godt ind i rollen og i måden, vi arbejder med support hos vores kunder.


Vores løfte til dig

  • Vi giver dig spændende kundeopgaver, mulighed for faglig og personlig udvikling samt et stærkt netværk på tværs af kunder og kolleger
  • Vi investerer i dig og betaler for kurser og certificeringer, så du kan holde dig fagligt skarp
  • Vi prioriterer fællesskabet – vores medarbejderklub arrangerer løbende sociale aktiviteter som go-kart, escape room og fælles arrangementer
  • Vi står bag dig og er der, når du har brug for det – med opbakning og sparring fra både din leder, dine kolleger og din account manager
  • Vi tilbyder fast løn, pension, sundhedsforsikring, bonus samt bruttolønsordning


Hvis det lyder som noget for dig

Så glæder vi os til at høre fra dig. Har du spørgsmål til rollen eller hverdagen som konsulent hos Mansoft, er du altid velkommen til at kontakte din nærmeste leder, Bo Widal, på 31 62 72 52.

4432273456,https://dk.linkedin.com/jobs/view/head-of-clinical-innovation-at-aisel-health-4432273456?position=2&pageNum=10&refId=pmzt1fCmv9B0JzIQPBV3bw%3D%3D&trackingId=eX4iVZlN8FEG9%2FFmROPdSg%3D%3D,Head of Clinical Innovation,Aisel Health,Denmark,"Om rollen

Som Head of Clinical Innovation bliver du den kliniske stemme i Aisel. Du står med det ene ben i klinikken og det andet i produktet, og du sikrer, at det vi udvikler, rent faktisk virker i en travl klinisk hverdag — ikke bare på papiret. Du får en central rolle med reel indflydelse på, hvad vi bygger, og hvordan vi gør det.
Dine opgaver
Oversætte klinisk hverdag til konkrete produktkrav - og være klinikernes advokat internt.
Arbejde tæt sammen med klinikker: forstå deres arbejdsgange, validere løsninger og drive adoption.
Eje den kliniske kvalitet og sikkerhed i produktet - notater, attester og erklæringer.
Designe og drive klinisk validering og effektmåling, fx dokumenteret tidsbesparelse ved AI-assisteret dokumentation.
Udvikle det kliniske indhold - skabeloner og logik for attester/erklæringer - sammen med produkt- og udviklingsteamet.
Være Aisels ansigt udadtil i sundhedssektoren: oplæg, netværk og relationer.
Holde os på rette side af faglige standarder og regulatoriske krav (journalføring, GDPR).
Vi forestiller os, at du
Har en klinisk baggrund (læge, psykiater, psykolog, sygeplejerske el.lign.) og kender det danske sundhedsvæsen indefra.
Har mærket dokumentationsbyrden på egen krop og brænder for at løse den.
Er nysgerrig på teknologi og AI og har lyst til at forme et produkt fra de tidlige stadier.
Kan bygge bro mellem klinikere og et produkt- og udviklingsteam.
Er selvkørende, hands-on og trives i et tidligt startup-miljø med højt tempo.
Taler og skriver dansk flydende og kan begå dig på engelsk.
Det er et plus, hvis du
Har erfaring fra psykiatrien.
Har arbejdet med produktudvikling, health-tech eller digitalisering af sundhedsvæsenet.
Har et stærkt netværk i sektoren.
Det tilbyder vi
Chancen for reelt at forme et produkt, der gør en forskel i klinikeres hverdag.
En central rolle med stor indflydelse og korte beslutningsveje i et tidligt team.
Løn og medejerskab efter kvalifikationer

4432253330,https://dk.linkedin.com/jobs/view/souschef-2-er-at-normal-a-s-4432253330?position=5&pageNum=10&refId=pmzt1fCmv9B0JzIQPBV3bw%3D%3D&trackingId=sCBoM%2Bxlakyf%2BP4HUKsV6Q%3D%3D,Souschef/2'er,NORMAL A/S,"Copenhagen Municipality, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark","Et souschef/2'ersjob i NORMAL er både for dig, der vil være en endnu dygtigere souschef eller måske drømmer om at blive butikschef.

Som souschef/2'er i NORMAL får du nemlig mulighed for at komme med på holdet i en organisation hvor der er plads til alle der deler vores ambition om at vi altid kan gøre det lidt bedre, og så er det bare en bonus der er højt til loftet og godt humør. Og nå ja, så kan du også komme med på vores interne souschefsuddannelse.
Om jobbet
Som souschef/2'er får du en nøglerolle i butikken og i arbejdet med at give kunderne en unik indkøbsoplevelse.

Sammen med butikschefen er det din opgave at skabe solid udvikling og indtjening i butikken samt sikre engagerede medarbejdere. Vi trætter dig ikke med lange manualer og stram central styring. Til gengæld forventer vi, at du som souschef sammen med butikschefen tager ansvar for salget, butiksstandarden og ledelse af teamet.
Lønnen er fastsat efter overenskomst.
Dine Ansvarsområder
Sætte kunderne i fokus, så de altid føler sig velkomne og får lyst til at komme igen.
Motivere og coache medarbejdere i et åbent og humørfyldt miljø.
Sikre en høj butiksstandard med fokus på salg og indtjening.
Opbygge en salgsklar butik, hvor du hele tiden har fokus på inspirerende vareoplæg.
Nå de budgetterede mål for butikken.
Sikre, at butikken arbejder effektivt med driften og salget.
Dine Kvalifikationer
Du har erfaring fra detailhandlen, og evt. gerne ambitioner om at blive butikschef en gang i fremtiden.
Du brænder for butiksdrift og købmandskab så vi sikrer en høj butiksstandard.
Du skaber gode kundeoplevelser, så vores kunder får lyst til at komme igen.
Du arbejder selvstændigt, er flittig og har styr på detaljerne.
Du er synlig og går forrest i hverdagen – med højt humør – og kan omsætte tanke til handling.
Du er ikke bange for at tage initiativ.
Kontakt Og Ansøgning
Vi afholder løbende interviews og ansætter, når vi har fundet den rette kandidat.

If so, Copenhagen Infrastructure Partners (CIP) is currently looking for an Analyst to join our Energy Management team based in Copenhagen.
Team

As an Analyst you will work in our Hyperscaler CoE & Business Development team within Energy Management in CIP. This team brings deep knowledge across both mature and emerging renewable energy markets and technologies. The team plays an important role in supporting CIP throughout the full lifecycle of renewable energy investments, including contributing insights and analytics during key transaction processes and supporting the commercial offtake and risk exposure of the Funds and their projects. The team’s work helps ensure that energy market dynamics are well reflected in investment decisions, supporting value creation and effective risk management across the portfolio.
Role

This is an opportunity to work at the intersection of renewable energy markets, analytics, and technology in a market-facing team with broad exposure across the CIP platform. You will work on real business problems with direct relevance to how CIP identifies opportunities, supports commercial decision-making, and builds scalable tools and processes across the platform. Rather than supporting from the sidelines, you will take real ownership of workstreams and contribute to projects that are used by stakeholders across the business.

For the right candidate, this role offers a steep learning curve, meaningful responsibility, and close collaboration with experienced colleagues and senior stakeholders, in one of the most important sectors at the heart of the energy transition.
The position is part-time and based in our headquarters in NordØ. The workload will vary depending on ongoing projects, with flexibility to structure your time according to your schedule.
Responsibilities will consist of but are not limited to:
Support commercial decision-making through building and maintaining Power BI dashboards and reporting tools,
Structure and manage datasets for market intelligence and pipeline tracking,
Support development of matching logic between projects and potential offtake opportunities,
Assist in preparation of materials for internal decision-making and stakeholder dialogues,
Contribute to system design discussions and documentation,
Manage smaller workstreams within larger initiatives.
Role development opportunities will include but are not limited to:
End-to-end delivery responsibility for smaller internal business development and analytics projects,
Direct collaboration with senior leaders and stakeholders across the CIP platform,
Opportunities to present data-, AI- and technology-focused business development and improvement ideas to senior leaders across CIP Platform Solutions,
Ownership of recurring market intelligence, pipeline tracking, and decision-support reporting across the platform.
You

We are looking for Copenhagen-based students finishing their bachelor’s or in the early stage of their Master’s. Relevant educational focuses include business analytics, AI, data science, software/technology, or similar quantitative fields from DTU, ITU, KU, CBS, or equivalent institutions.

You should have a solid technical foundation, be comfortable working directly with data and digital tools, and be motivated by building structured and automated solutions to real business problems. We value this alongside commercial curiosity, structured thinking, and an interest in renewable energy.
We look for the following in a candidate:
Structured, detail-oriented, and able to work from problem statement to practical solution,
Good communication and collaboration skills, with the ability to engage both business and technical stakeholders,
Able to translate business needs into analytical, technical, and product-like solutions,
Comfortable managing their own work and taking ownership of building and improving tools,
Solid academic and/or practical experience within relevant data, analytics, or technology fields.
Strong candidates will also bring hands-on technology and data skills:
Experienced within data structuring and data modelling, with the ability to turn messy inputs into usable datasets and scalable logic,
Experience building solutions yourself in tools such as Power BI, Power Apps, Power Automate, Excel, SQL, Python, or similar technologies,
Ability and motivation to develop working prototypes, internal tools, automations, and decision-support products,
Solid understanding of how data flows across systems, including data capture, transformation, logic, reporting, and user-facing applications,
A solid understanding of the role and practical deployment opportunities of AI in tools, systems, and business applications,
Curiosity around system design, product thinking, and how technology can be used to solve real commercial problems in a scalable way.

Please note that we do not offer a remote working opportunity, thus you must be a Danish resident or an upcoming student enrolled in a Danish university to be eligible for this role. Students on exchange in Denmark are not considered eligible for this position.
The process

Please apply online as soon as possible as applications will be processed on an ongoing basis. For questions related to the role, please reach out to our Talent Acquisition team at recruitment@cipfs.com. We do not include CV's and Cover Letters sent by email.

As we continue to improve our candidate experience, diversity and avoid unconscious bias, please avoid having a photo in your CV. We commit to an inclusive recruitment process and equality for all applicants.

Please be aware that we will not accept speculative CV submissions for any of our roles from recruitment agencies, and any unsolicited candidate submissions will be exempt from any payment expectations and ownership.
Building value that matters

Founded in 2012, Copenhagen Infrastructure Partners P/S (CIP) today is the world’s largest dedicated fund manager within greenfield renewable energy investments and a global leader in offshore wind. To date, CIP has developed and constructed more than +50 greenfield renewable projects around the world. CIP is developing a greenfield project portfolio of ~160GW total, and ~16 GW is in operation or under construction. CIP manages 13 funds and has to date raised approximately EUR ~35 billion for investments in energy and associated infrastructure from more than ~200 international institutional investors.

CIP has a multinational team of ~500 full-time employees with extensive experience and knowledge within the fields of renewable energy project development and regulated infrastructure. In addition, CIP has a broad range of competencies within corporate finance, mergers & acquisitions, engineering, construction, project development, and project management.

CIP has built a prominent investment team capable of originating proprietary investment opportunities and executing projects and investments based on the team’s industrial background, large industrial network, and extensive experience in structuring, executing, and managing investments within energy infrastructure. Headquartered in Copenhagen, CIP also operates from key locations across the globe, including offices in Hamburg, Munich, London, Utrecht, New York, Tokyo, Melbourne, Luxembourg, Kuala Lumpur, Seoul and Singapore. In addition, CIP has 17 local project offices globally.
For more information, visit www.cip.com.
